title: Product Engineer/Tech Lead - Agentic Recruitment OS
description:
Product Engineer/Tech Lead (Fullstack) - Agentic Recruiting OS



Location: Remote (preferably any timezone with ? 4 h overlap to CET) 

Engagement: Full-time, 40 h/week 

Compensation: USD 900 - 2'300 base/month + Performance Bonus (USD 100 – 250 per successful placement made through the system you ship) + path to equity/co-founder stake for the right person 

Start: ASAP

 

Intro

I'm a headhunter. I built a 100 000-line AI-native CRM/ATS that is already running my own agency. Now I'm turning it into the first true Agentic Operating System for recruiting, a SaaS where customers don't subscribe to software, they subscribe to a digital workforce.

The MVP isn't a prototype. It's almost production-ready. I need a technical partner to take the wheel: architecture, multi-tenancy, agent orchestration, customer onboarding, the works. You will be the technical lead from day one, no PMs above you, no Jira theatre, no committees.

If you are a "9-to-5, Slack-status-yellow" developer, please don't apply. If you've shipped a SaaS, live in Cursor/Codex/Claude Code and routinely have 5+ agents running in parallel, keep reading.



What's already built

Not a green-field gig. The foundation is real, battle-tested, and producing revenue:

• React 18 + TypeScript frontend — Vite, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, 5 languages, full PDF pipeline

• Supabase backend — 147 migrations, 36 tables, 172 RLS policies, role-based access control

• pgvector + HNSW cosine indices for semantic candidate ? job matching

• 48 Edge Functions (~18k LoC) — each effectively a ready-to-wire agent tool: CV parsing, predictive market radar, deep-reasoning seniority engine (L1–L6), employer-conflict detection, exposé generation, public job feed, full ATS flow + ~35 more

• Live in production today — full Candidate / Client / Job / Pipeline / Recruiting / Tasks / Applications management, AI Matching Engine, Predictive Market Radar, Career-Page backend, full CV pipeline

Quality bar: TypeScript end-to-end, generated DB types, RLS-enforced security. Not perfect — but a working foundation.

The Mission, in three phases

1. SaaS-ify the Beast — multi-tenant SaaS with org/workspace isolation, Stripe billing, per-tenant secrets, hardened RLS

2. Agent Orchestration — wrap the 48 Edge Functions in an agent runtime (your call: LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, Mastra, Inngest, custom). Long-running, queue-backed agent loops. MCP host so customers plug in their Slack / Calendar / Wiki / ATS (BYOT system) and our agents act on their behalf

3. Scale & Customer Wins — migration pipelines from Bullhorn, Vincere, Pipedrive, Recruitee, Excel. Multi-provider LLM routing (OpenAI + Anthropic + Gemini) with evals, fallbacks, and cost controls. WhatsApp +
 outreach automation

You own the product architecture and the technical roadmap. I bring product vision and customer pain — you bring the tech direction. Once we hire the next engineer, you lead them

Who you are

• Cracked builder. Shipped real products, ideally as a founder or first engineer. You don't need a ticket to know what to build next

• AI-native. Cursor is muscle memory. RAG, function calling, vector search, LLM evals — strong opinions on which model fits which task

• Strategic thinker. Ship fast where it doesn't matter; obsess over fundamentals where it does (security, multi-tenancy, data integrity)

• Proactive communicator. When my idea is wrong, you tell me — with reasoning, not opinions

• Bonus points: prior SaaS, Supabase + Edge Functions in production, built an agentic system before, speak German, or have HR-tech context

Why join

• Full ownership. Technical lead from day one. No micromanagement.

• Strong long-term upside, discussed individually with the right candidate after a few weeks of working together

• You win when I win. Performance bonus tied to every placement my agency closes through your system, every month, before SaaS revenue starts

• Real distribution. I'm the first paying customer. Warm pipeline of befriended agencies ready for closed beta

• Coding-agent-native War Room. Multiple parallel agent instances are how we ship — no need to convince anyone AI-assisted dev is legit

• No-bullshit timeline. Closed beta this summer, public launch in autumn, fundraising thereafter (2027) 

Stack - open to evolution

The current stack got us to production. It is not sacred. We are explicitly open to a different stack and architecture if you can argue why it makes us faster, more scalable, or more cost-efficient.

Today: React + TS + Vite + Tailwind + shadcn/ui · Supabase (Postgres, Auth, Storage, Deno Edge Functions) · pgvector + HNSW · RLS-based access control · single LLM provider with embedding pre-selection + caching · commercial search/scraping APIs + Google Maps · custom Postgres job-state pattern.

You'll build, evolve, or replace: multi-tenancy + Stripe billing · agent orchestration runtime with observability + human-in-the-loop · MCP host · multi-provider LLM routing · WhatsApp +
 outreach · possibly Next.js for the public side · migration pipelines.

Non-negotiables: solid security, clean multi-tenancy, shipping speed. Everything else is up for debate.

title: Virtual Assistant – Cold Calling & Lead Generation (Real Estate)
description:
We are a U.S.-based real estate investment company looking for a sharp, reliable Virtual Assistant to support our wholesaling and lead generation operations.

What You’ll Be Doing:

 • Cold calling motivated seller leads from provided lists

 • Skip tracing and data entry (BatchLeads, spreadsheets)

 • Following up with leads and updating a CRM/call log

 • Light administrative tasks as needed

Requirements:

 • Stable, high-speed internet connection (required — non-negotiable)

 • Strong English communication skills, both spoken and written

 • At least 1 year of cold calling experience, preferably in real estate or sales

 • Comfortable with rejection and able to stay consistent on high call volume

 • Familiarity with tools like BatchLeads, Google Sheets, or any dialer is a plus

 • Available during U.S. Eastern or Central business hours

What We’re Looking For in a Person:

 • Self-motivated and disciplined — you don’t need to be micromanaged

 • Coachable and open to feedback

 • Detail-oriented with clean notes and follow-up habits

 • Long-term mindset — we want to grow with the right person

To Apply: Record a short 1–2 minute voice or video introduction. Tell us your cold calling experience and why you’re a great fit. Applications without this will not be considered.

title: Client Relationship Manager
description:
$3500-$4500* per month depending on experience and skill level FULL TIME (40 hrs / week)

Fully remote role

Flexible working hours that overlap with our working hours in USA Eastern Standard Time (EST).

Paid Vacation, Sick, Holiday, and every other Friday off paid for full time

Bonus “P4P” Incentive Pay plan.

Professional growth opportunities



At Fiorilli Construction, the team are dedicated, passionate, creative, hard-working commercial construction professionals who believe in providing outstanding services along with a great built environment product. They love the construction industry, are exceptional at what they do, and provide S.M.A.R.T. solutions to their clients’ construction anxieties within 24 hours.



They have integrity in all they do and believe in contributing to an amazing work environment by utilizing cutting-edge cool services. They live by the philosophy of work hard, play hard. The team are passionate about their lifestyle and creating the “Fiorilli Family” experience for both their co-workers and clients.



At the end of the day, Building Synergistic Authentic Relationships™ (BSAR) is their #1 priority. Working at Fiorilli Construction is not just a job, but a passion and they bring that to each project that they do.



Fiorilli Construction’s commitment is to an amazing work environment, and innovative services. They espouse a "work hard, play hard" philosophy, fostering a family-like experience for their team and clients.



Looking for a dynamic and rewarding role where your skills truly make a difference?



The Company’s primary goal for this role is to utilize your proactive, relationship-focused, and results-driven approach as our Client Relationship Manager. You will work closely with the Director of Client Relations, the President, and the Sales and Marketing team to identify great clients, generate new opportunities, and support Fiorilli’s growth and market expansion.



We are looking for an assertive, energetic, and high-performing professional who is comfortable networking, prospecting, and engaging fluidly in the construction industry. Ideal candidates must be passionate about delivering outstanding customer service while demonstrating strong accountability, initiative, and professionalism.



We invite you to join Fiorilli Construction as a Client Relationship Manager. This is not your typical sales position — in this role, you are a key driver of the sales pipeline and a critical contributor to company revenue and client satisfaction. You will play an instrumental role in ensuring a seamless sales process and exceptional client experience.



Every week, your performance will be reflected through your outreach, pipeline activity, opportunity generation, and relationship-building efforts. You will be responsible for managing sales data, supporting campaigns, coordinating client engagement activities, and driving meaningful business development results.



In your role as Client Relationship Manager, you will be more than just a salesperson — you will be a trusted relationship builder and a vital support to Fiorilli’s sales engine. Your efforts will help create strong opportunities, nurture meaningful client relationships, and contribute directly to the company’s success.



Job Title: Client Relationship Manager



Job Overview:

The Client Relationship Manager will be primarily responsible for generating new business opportunities through proactive lead generation, outreach, and relationship-building. This role requires a motivated, results-driven individual who is comfortable with consistent client engagement, high-volume outreach, and managing sales activities across multiple channels.



The Client Relationship Manager will play a crucial role in expanding Fiorilli Construction’s customer base, nurturing long-term relationships, and supporting overall sales and marketing efforts.



Key Responsibilities & Accountabilities:

1. Lead Generation implementation:

a. Utilize a diverse range of sources including referrals, website analytics, social media, events/tradeshows, telemarketing,
 blasts, mailers, searches, and industry news to call upon and generate sales leads.

b. Compile and pre-qualify lists of potential clients based on specific criteria such as market segments, geographic fit, project scope capabilities, and decision-making authority.



2. Business Development:

a. Source and call upon qualified lead gen prospects and actively contribute to the development of sales strategies and plans in order to reach and exceed your sale breakpoint requirements.

b. Act as the primary interface in sales efforts, spearheading calls, outreach, and touches to facilitate new sales opportunities.

c. Develop strategy, vision, and business planning process for market segments assigned.

d. Maintain and follow up on all proposals, bids, leads, and negotiation reviews of potential sales awards.

e. Offer technical expertise and advice during the entire sales process.



3. Sales & Marketing:

a. Work towards achieving individual sales goals, AS DEFINED under the Work for Hire Service Agreement payment terms in your Pay for Performance (P4P) Individual Annual Sales Target Goals BREAKPOINT and performance tiers, contributing to the overall sales targets of the company.

b. Utilize CRM tools like HubSpot for tracking potentials, opportunities, campaigns, and touches, ensuring efficient management of sales processes. Working with marketing to campaign in your market segments assigned.

c. Assist in sales and marketing reporting, forecasting, and process management to drive efficiency and effectiveness.



4. Client Relationship Management:

a. Focus on driving quality business and developing assigned markets by cultivating long term relationships for repeat business.

b. Spearhead calls, outreach, and touches to set up and facilitate new sales opportunities.

c. Manage and curate lists of target client prospects.

d. Arrange calls and meetings with clients and prospects.

e. Cultivate new and ongoing business prospects.

f. Attend virtual client meetings, events, and conferences to promote the company.

g. Maintain regular contact to ensure satisfaction with project progress and results.



5. Customer Engagement & Relationship Management:

a. Initiate and maintain contact with potential clients through various communication channels such as calls,
s, LinkedIn, and personalized visits.

b. Implement drip campaigns and other nurturing strategies to keep leads engaged and progress them through the sales pipeline.

c. Manage and curate lists of target client prospects, arranging calls and meetings to further business opportunities.

d. Attend client meetings, events, and conferences to promote the company's offerings and ensure client satisfaction with project progress and results.



Requirements:

a. Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or related field.

b. Preferred experience in engineering, architecture, construction, sales, business development, or lead generation.

c. Proficiency in CRM software, marketing automation tools, and other relevant technology platforms.

d. Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.

e. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.

f. Ability to work independently and as part of a team.



First Year Outcomes:

a. Source and sell designated amount of new business.

b. Become proficient in HubSpot CRM for company-wide tracking.

c. Create specified number of new client opportunities.



Long Term Career Goals:

a. Strive to establish strategic career goals that lead to long-term growth and positioning within the company.



What’s in it for you?



$3500-$4500* per month FULL TIME

Full-time and fully remote role

Flexible working hours that are consistent or overlap with our working hours in USA Eastern Standard Time (EST).

Paid Vacation Time Off (PVTO) up to a total of 80 hours PTO time as accrued is available annually

Paid Sick Time Off (PSTO) up to twenty-four (24) hours of sick time is available annually, with rollover

Eligibility to enroll immediately in Fiorilli Construction’s “P4P” Incentive Pay plan – TO EARN BONUSES OF 10% - 20% more than you base compensation base on YOUR performance.

Concentrated Time Off (CTO) Work Shifts (aka ‘8.5/80’ Plan). The team follows a 2-week Work Shift (80 hours, 10 working days) that incorporates 8.5-hour days, Monday through Thursday (adding 2 hrs. weekly), with every other Friday requiring only a 4-hour shift (1/2 day). That’s approximately 26 Friday afternoons OFF!

Holiday pay - all major USA holidays

Professional growth opportunities



*Please note that this is a gross salary and that you are responsible for any tax-related procedures in your country of residence. We kindly ask you to apply only if you are happy to work remotely as an independent contractor.



As they grow as a company, it has become more and more important to explicitly define the core values from which they develop their culture, brand, and business strategies. These are the five core values that we live by



The Fiorilli Construction team has local professionals as well as international virtual service providers. The company offers in-person, hybrid, remote, and flex work policies that allow for maximum flexibility in today's work environment to get the results that are needed for everyone's success.



They leverage great vision, core values, metrics that are measurable in each role, accountability for all, core values, right people in the right seats, great problem solving, systems and processes that are followed by all at what they call 80% strong to achieve the maximum results for the greater good, understanding that the whole is greater than the individual and "we are greater than I".



This job opening is actively seeking the perfect candidate and will close once found. To ensure your application is considered, take the time to carefully read the job description and submit a high-quality application that stands out.



The hiring process for this position will include the following steps:



1.Provide your CV (resume) - provide hyperlink

2.Recording a short video introduction of yourself noting why you are best suited for this position – provide hyperlink

3.If shortlisted, completing behavior survey and job related test tasks

4.Attending video interviews on teams to discuss your previous experience and the new role in more detail.



To make sure you receive updates on your application please add Fiona Ingram ---------- to your application
 address book.

title: Urgent Hiring: Senior Insurance Virtual Assistant (EZLynx) – Full Time | Remote
description:
Urgent Hiring: Senior Insurance Virtual Assistant (EZLynx) – Full Time | Remote



We are a U.S.-based insurance business seeking an experienced Virtual Assistant with strong hands-on knowledge of EZLynx and familiarity with tools like GoHighLevel (GHL) to support backend operations and day-to-day system management.



This is a senior-level, full-time role suited for someone who can step into an active system environment, identify gaps, and implement improvements with minimal supervision.



About the Role



Following a recent system transition, the focus is on improving data accuracy, refining workflows, and strengthening overall operations. We are looking for someone who understands how insurance operations function within an Agency Management System (AMS) and how it connects with other internal tools.

Required Experience

Experience working with systems used to manage client data and internal workflows



Strong understanding of:

The distinction between an Agency Management System (AMS) and other operational tools

This is not an entry-level position. Candidates are expected to work independently without formal training on core systems.



Preferred Qualifications

Work Schedule

Remote



What We’re Looking For

title: YouTube Tutorial Creator (Screen Recording, Long-Term Work, High Volume)
description:
Hi everyone,



I’m currently looking for multiple freelancers to create short how-to / explainer videos for YouTube. I’m already working with a small team and looking to bring on several more people to increase output.



This is a simple screen-recording role where you explain how to do something step by step throughout the entire video.



We work with many different tools and software, some examples of tools and topics other freelancers have covered are:

- Canva: How to add a shadow to an image in Canva

- Shopify: How to add products in Shopify

- Mailchimp: How to find campaign ID in Mailchimp



There are thousands of topics available, and this is intended to be long-term, ongoing work.



How the workflow works:

- You will be assigned one software at a time (e.g. 
, Airtable, Asana, Klaviyo, etc.)

- You will need to spend some time familiarising yourself with that software before creating tutorials

- Each software includes a list of approximately 100 to 500 tutorial topics

- Your role is to work through as many topics as possible, ideally completing the full list

- Once finished, you will be assigned a new software and repeat the process



What the work involves:

- Recording short tutorials (usually 1–4 minutes, sometimes longer)

- Explaining clearly while navigating the software

- Basic editing (cutting mistakes, trimming pauses)

- Creating a simple thumbnail using a provided template I've created in Canva (quick and easy, mostly adding text)



Requirements:

- English fluency (minimum intermediate level) - this role is not suitable for freelancers with broken or difficult-to-understand English

- Clear audio quality

- A decent microphone with minimal background noise (noise reduction tools are fine, I recommend Krisp, SteelseriesGG, or Nvidia Broadcast for background noise removal)

- Ability to explain clearly and naturally (sounding confident is great!)

- Reliable and consistent, able to meet deadlines and communicate properly



Pay:

- $1 per approved video

- No cap on output

- You can produce 30 videos or 300 videos per week, completely up to you how many videos you want to create

- Please include how many videos you think you can produce weekly in your application



Time expectations (for transparency):

These are simple videos. Once you are familiar with the process, it is realistic to produce around 10 completed videos per hour (approximately $10 per hour), depending on tutorial lenght and complexity.



Example breakdown:

~20 minutes recording

~20 minutes editing

~10 minutes thumbnails

~10 minutes buffer (extra edits, research, etc.)



This is just to give an idea of the workflow.



How to apply:

Please send a short sample how-to video created by you.



- It can be simple (e.g. how to change your desktop background)

- A more complex tutorial is preferred if possible

- Attach the file or send a Google Drive link (make sure it is set to public)



This will be used to evaluate:

- Your English level

- Your clarity when explaining

- Your on-screen presentation

Networking Follow-up & Relationship Support

Take photos or scans of business cards you send after events and: add contacts to your CRM, send connection requests via LinkedIn and/or social platforms, and send a pre-approved follow-up
 (e.g., “Loved meeting you at the luncheon, let’s connect for a 1:1”).

Track responses inside your
 and CRM, flag those who reply, and help set up calls/meetings or prompt you when a handwritten note or personal message is needed.

 & Calendar Support

Log into your

Help with calendar coordination so you can move from event to event and call to call without worrying about the before/after admin work.

Social Media & Online Presence

Review and help optimize your current platforms—including your personal
 page, the Generations Group page, and any other relevant profiles—to ensure your branding, photos, and messaging are consistent and professional.

Begin posting and nurturing engagement for your Generations Group page and other channels so that your presence reflects the scale of your relationships and experience, even though you don’t currently post much yourself.

Use examples like Gentle Hearts and similar senior-focused sites as inspiration for Solutions for Generations, ensuring your real estate business and senior solutions cross-link in a way that “all sings together” without overcomplication.

Senior Solutions & Resource Coordination

Support you in building out the infrastructure for Solutions for Generations, including resources pages, referral listings (e.g., senior living communities, services), and integration of your real estate offerings as part of a full-service transition solution.

Operational Efficiency & Proactivity

Ask clarifying questions about your networking, events, and systems so follow-up is personalized (e.g., tagging by event, prompting you for specific personal touches), not just generic data entry.

Preferred Qualities

Has prior experience working with real estate professionals and is comfortable learning and working inside CRMs like Buffini (with access to any training resources you provide).

Has strong written and spoken English, with minimal language barrier, reflecting your very relational, high-touch communication style.

Is comfortable inside
, social media, and basic website platforms (e.g., HomeSmart pages, GoDaddy-connected sites, and simple landing page builders).

Is proactive, a self-starter, and comfortable taking direction like “Here’s what I want, here it is, please do it,” without creating more work for you.

Respects the separation of financials (you already have a bookkeeper and QuickBooks handled) and focuses on operational, marketing, and relationship-support tasks.

title: Website Production & Quality Specialist
description:
About the Role



We are hiring a Website Production & Quality Specialist to review, refine, and finalize websites before they are delivered to customers. Your responsibility is to ensure every site meets our internal quality checklist and standards for layout, structure, clarity, and consistency.



This is a hands-on role inside our WYSIWYG editor. You will not simply point out issues. You will fix them.



We are looking for someone who is able to commit to this role full-time and treat it as their primary professional focus.



This is a remote contract role.



What You’ll Do



• Review completed websites against a defined quality checklist

• Work directly inside our editor to correct layout, spacing, formatting, and structural issues

• Ensure headings, sections, links, and metadata are implemented correctly

• Improve unclear or awkward copy where needed

• Ensure consistency across all pages within a site

• Perform final quality checks before sites are considered complete

• Identify recurring issues and suggest improvements to internal processes



Required Skills and Experience



• Exceptional attention to detail. You naturally notice spacing inconsistencies, formatting errors, broken links, and structural issues

• Direct experience using WYSIWYG website editors or builders

• Confidence making layout and content edits independently

• Strong written English and ability to improve clarity

• Ability to work methodically through structured checklists without missing small issues

• Availability to commit full-time to this role as your primary professional focus



Nice to Have



• Experience reviewing small business websites

• Background in website production, publishing, or editorial roles

• Basic understanding of SEO structure and page hierarchy



Working Hours and Location



• Remote role based in the Philippines

• Core working hours aligned with EST

• First two weeks of training may require availability during PST hours

title: Senior Amazon OA Sourcer ? Wholesale ? Manager Track + Profit Share
description:
I'm hiring the person who will eventually run my sourcing department and share in the profits of this business. This is a career, not a gig. 



There will be zero basic training. You need to walk in with the fundamentals locked in. You should already know how to calculate discounted gift cards into your cost, what a BOGO is and how to account for it, how to verify a US website or supplier without relying on Trustpilot, how to find discount codes, how to use Keepa Product Finder to locate out of stock items and suppressed variations, and which sites are bulk friendly and which are not. If any of that sounds unfamiliar, this is not the right role.



When I send you a task you haven't done before, you can figure it out. AI, Google, YouTube, courses, then you ask me. You exhaust every option before coming to me. When you come to me for help it's "I used these resources, and tried this. It didn't work, do you have any advice or resources I should look at?" It should never be "I can't do that" If that doesn't describe you, please don't apply. I expect you to be the business operator in 3 months. 





The path is clear. For the first three weeks you're doing OA sourcing. You'll hit the ground running with a full brand list and corresponding websites. I expect at least $30,000(only 300 units at $100 a pop) in spend month 1.  Asins that are instock, that I can buy, and  I actually spend money on. Week four we align on wholesale sourcing, lead generation, and what that transition looks like. After probation you hire and train your OA replacement, move into wholesale, and start building the team underneath you. 



I'm focused on higher ASP items($70 buy cost minimum, 100+ preferred). If something moves 50+ units a month I need 30% ROI minimum. If it moves 100+ that floor drops to 25%.



You'll be working in SmartScout, Keepa, and whatever calculator you prefer (SellerAmp, RevSeller, AZInsight, etc.) along with Google Sheets and Slack. Time Doctor is also required from day one. I've had a previous bad hire and this protects both of us. If your work quality is strong I'm never looking at it. 



The person I'm looking for lives and breathes Ecommerce and Amazon. You were probably under appreciated in your last role, had to carry your teammates to quota, and never got to recruit the A players or get the pay you deserved. You want a challenging, stable, high income position where your ceiling is determined by your output, not someone else's budget. No need for multiple jobs. I want 3 more tea
mbers that you manage by the end of the year. 



You have a minimum of 4 years full time Amazon sourcing experience with a proven track record. You can show me real numbers and have references. You continue learning on your own through YouTube, courses, AI tools, whatever keeps you sharp. I will pay for courses and tools you need to do your job better. I won't keep you doing the same thing, and you'll have space to fail, grow, and learn new skills. 



You have experience hiring, managing, and firing tea
mbers. You've created SOPs. You understand why process matters when you're building a team.



The schedule is 8 hours a day, 5 days a week. Show up on time. We will meet every single day for the first month. After that, meetings become less frequent as trust is built. 



This is a long term play. I'm investing time and resources in whoever fills this seat, and I expect you to perform and hope you'll stay long term. Ideally 5 years or longer. In return you'll get annual raises, performance bonuses, 13th month salary, and profit share once you step into the manager role. There is zero ceiling on your income potential here. What you earn is directly tied to how much value you create.





Tell me about your amazon and sourcing experience with real examples. Walk me through how you'd calculate a deal using discounted gift cards and a BOGO offer. Explain how you verify a US supplier without Trustpilot. Show me how you've used Keepa Product Finder to find opportunity. Then tell me what downside is, how you think about risk, how that determines how many units you buy, what the biggest types of risk are in OA sourcing, and when it's worth going deep on a single ASIN. Finally, tell me what makes a lead great for OA, what makes a lead great for wholesale? and how those two answers are different from each other. What if an order gets canceled from a retailer? How would we circumvent/ get past that? 



To apply, send me a video under 10 minutes. No scripts, no polish. I want to hear how you think. Do not give me answers you think I want to hear, just give me your thoughts. Applications without a video will not be reviewed.

title: Executive Assistant to the CEO
description:
APPLICANTS WHO APPLY THROUGH THE LINK BELOW WILL BE SCREENED





About Us:

gigCMO is a dynamic growth consultancy providing strategic marketing leadership and expertise to start-ups, scale-ups, and SMEs, propelling them toward profitable revenue growth. Nestled in the heart of the City of London, United Kingdom, we specialise in supporting B2B clients in expanding their reach domestically and internationally. Our dedicated team, operating under the banner of Magnacca International, is strategically based in the Philippines. We chose this location for its pool of talent, strong social skills, enthusiasm, and unwavering loyalty.



Executive Assistant to the CEO



We are seeking a highly experienced Executive Assistant (EA) to provide dedicated, senior-level support to our CEO/Founder. This role is suited for candidates with extensive experience in high-pressure, fast-paced environments, especially those accustomed to working at the executive level in a corporate or professional services setting. The EA will ensure smooth operations by managing the CEO’s schedule, external communications, and high-level administrative tasks.



The ideal candidate will have a strong background in executive support, impeccable organisational skills, excellent communication abilities, and experience in managing high-level relationships both internally and externally. Professionalism, discretion, and proactive problem-solving are essential qualities for this role.



Key Responsibilities:



Executive Administrative Support:

Manage the CEO’s complex schedule with precision, including high-stakes meetings, international travel, and detailed itineraries.

Act as the primary gatekeeper for the CEO, handling all forms of communication, including
s, phone calls, and meeting requests.

Organise and prepare meeting agendas, documents, and reports for both internal and external meetings.

Handle confidential and time-sensitive information with the utmost discretion.

Draft and proofread professional communications on behalf of the CEO, ensuring clarity, accuracy, and appropriate tone.

External Stakeholder Management:

Serve as the first point of contact for external parties, representing the CEO in interactions with high-level stakeholders, including clients, partners, and board members.

Maintain and nurture professional relationships on behalf of the CEO, ensuring timely follow-up and proactive communication.

Project Coordination & Task Management:

Collaborate with the CEO on strategic initiatives, ensuring timely execution of tasks and deliverables.

Assist in managing ongoing projects by coordinating with internal teams and tracking progress.

Oversee special assignments that require high attention to detail and organisational oversight.

Document & Data Management:

Ensure the organisation of key documents and maintain efficient filing systems (both digital and physical), leveraging Microsoft SharePoint for data integrity and accessibility.

Prepare polished presentations, reports, and briefing materials for the CEO’s meetings with key stakeholders.

High-Level Communication and Meeting Coordination:

Manage and prioritise the CEO’s communications with high-profile contacts.

Coordinate video and phone conferences using platforms such as Microsoft Teams, Zoom, Google Meets or other online applications.

Ensure that follow-up actions fro
etings are captured and appropriately addressed.

Financial Oversight:

Manage basic financial administrative tasks, such as expense tracking, budget reconciliation, and vendor payments.

(Optional) QuickBooks proficiency is an advantage for light bookkeeping tasks.



Qualifications and Skills:

At least 6 years of experience as an Executive Assistant supporting C-suite executives, preferably in a corporate or professional services environment.

Exceptional organisational and time-management skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritise effectively.

Outstanding communication and interpersonal skills, with a professional and composed demeanour in dealing with high-level external contacts.

Mastery of Microsoft Office (especially advanced Word, Excel, PowerPoint), and familiarity with SharePoint for document management.

Proven ability to handle confidential information with absolute discretion.

Highly detail-oriented, proactive, and capable of working independently with minimal supervision.

Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to anticipate needs and act decisively.



Preferred Attributes:

Previous experience in a corporate secretary or similar role, providing support to high-level executives or board members.

Familiarity with UK and international business practices, including working across time zones and managing global relationships.

Flexible availability to accommodate business demands that may occasionally extend beyond standard working hours.



Compensation: Php 30,000 to Php 50,000 (depending on whether the applicant meets 100% of the requirements)



Application Requirements: This role demands a full-time commitment and is not compatible with another full-time job.

To be considered, kindly fill out the form using this link: 
/eatotheceo

****Only successful candidates will be contacted for interviews.*****



Note: This role requires exclusive dedication and cannot be combined with another full-time job. Only submissions meeting the specified requirements and demonstrating the ability to follow instructions will be considered. If you're prepared to surpass our expectations and contribute to our vibrant team, we eagerly anticipate receiving your application.

title: SEMrush Specialist (Remote)
description:
About Us:

gigCMO is a growth consultancy that provides strategic marketing leadership to start-ups, scale-ups, and SMEs. As part of our ongoing efforts to enhance our clients' digital presence and performance, we heavily rely on SEMrush for data-driven insights and strategy development. We are seeking a SEMrush Specialist to manage our SEMrush platform, analyse its data, and provide actionable recommendations to optimise our clients' digital strategies.





Job Summary:

We are looking for an experienced SEMrush Specialist to oversee the management and analysis of SEMrush data, offering insights and recommendations to various internal teams. This role is critical for developing and refining SEO and digital marketing strategies, based on in-depth analysis of SEMrush data. The ideal candidate will be highly proficient in using SEMrush and have a strong understanding of SEO, PPC, and competitive analysis.





Key Responsibilities:



Manage and monitor the SEMrush platform to track SEO performance, keyword rankings, and overall site health for our clients.

Analyse SEMrush data, providing detailed reports on SEO, PPC campaigns, competitor analysis, backlink profiles, and content performance.

Develop and recommend strategies for improving organic search rankings, increasing website traffic, and optimising digital marketing efforts based on SEMrush insights.

Collaborate with content, SEO, and marketing teams to align strategies with SEMrush data, ensuring all digital efforts are data-driven.

Conduct keyword research using SEMrush to identify high-potential keywords and provide recommendations for content optimisation.

Identify and track competitors' activities, providing insights on their strengths and weaknesses using SEMrush competitive analysis tools.

Stay up to date with SEMrush updates, new features, and industry trends to ensure we are maximising the platform's potential.

Generate regular reports on SEMrush performance metrics for different teams, including SEO health, keyword movements, and traffic changes.

title: Product Manager - SaaS
description:
gigCMO is a dynamic growth consultancy providing strategic marketing leadership and expertise to start-ups, scale-ups, and SMEs, propelling them toward profitable revenue growth. Nestled in the heart of the City of London, United Kingdom, we specialise in supporting B2B clients in expanding their reach domestically and internationally. Our dedicated team, operating under the banner of Magnacca International, is strategically based in the Philippines. We chose this location for its pool of talent, strong social skills, enthusiasm, and unwavering loyalty.



We are seeking a dedicated and dynamic Product Manager to lead and enhance our Digital Church Platform®. The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing the product development lifecycle, managing development resources effectively, and ensuring rigorous final testing processes before releasing software updates. You will also play a critical role in translating the strategic vision held by the founder into actionable roadmaps and product deliverables.



Key Responsibilities:



- Manage the Product Development (PD) pipeline, ensuring alignment with organisational goals.

- Prioritise and allocate development resources efficiently to align with strategic priorities.

- Conduct comprehensive final testing and quality assurance to guarantee smooth, error-free software updates before public release.

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to deliver product enhancements that meet user needs and drive platform adoption.

- Communicate clearly and proactively with stakeholders regarding project timelines, milestones, and release schedules.

- Translate strategic vision and high-level ideas from leadership into actionable product roadmaps, user stories, and specifications. 

- Facilitate clear and consistent communication between non-technical stakeholders and technical teams, ensuring all parties are aligned.

- Set up and manage internal systems (e.g. Basecamp and HubSpot) to track backlog, sprints, and milestones.

- Gather feedback from churches and users to shape future features and improvements. 

- Monitor product performance or user feedback loops to inform decisions and prioritisation. 

- Monitor incoming requests via helpdesk and chat channels, triage issues, and ensure timely resolution either directly or by coordinating with the development team.



Qualifications:



- Proven experience in Product Management, ideally within digital platforms or SaaS environments.

- Strong organisational skills with an ability to manage multiple development priorities simultaneously.

- Exceptional attention to detail, particularly in software testing and quality assurance.

- Demonstrable capability in managing software product lifecycles effectively.

- Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, able to engage stakeholders at various levels.

- Previous experience in digital community platforms or faith-based digital services is advantageous but not essential.

- Experience writing product requirements, specs, and user stories to support development.

- Understanding of user needs in faith-based or nonprofit contexts, particularly around compliance, admin, or community tools, is highly desirable. 

- A mindset geared toward scalability, continuous improvement, and hands-on problem solving in an early-stage SaaS environment.

title: LinkedIn Engagement and Community Specialist
description:

munity Manager with a proven track record to lead and enhance our social media presence on LinkedIn. title: Office Network & Infrastructure Setup (AD, VLANs, Wi-Fi, VoIP, CCTV)
description:
Objective



Design and implement a secure, segmented, and reliable office network with Active Directory, proper VLAN separation, wireless access, VoIP, and CCTV—ready for day-to-day operations and future scaling.



Existing Environment

Servers

Dell PowerEdge R630 ? to host Primary Domain Controller (VM)

Dell PowerEdge R220 ? to be configured as Secondary Domain Controller (physical)

2 × Huawei Servers (Hyper-V)

Each has ~5 VMs (development use)

Must remain operational and integrated into the network

Network & Security

Sophos Firewall

1 × Fiber Internet Connection

Switching

1 × 24-port Managed Switch (Core/Main)

2 × 8-port Managed Switches (Room switches)

Wireless

3 × Wi-Fi Access Points (PoE)

Endpoints

10 × Grandstream IP Phones (PC pass-through)

4 × IP Cameras

13 Users total

3 Management

8 Developers

2 Support

Scope of Work

1) Active Directory Deployment

Install and configure:

Primary DC (VM) on R630

Secondary DC (physical) on R220

Configure:

DNS (AD-integrated)


Replication (verify with repadmin)

Create:

Basic OU structure (Management, Developers, Support, Servers)

Initial user accounts and groups (optional but preferred)

Harden DCs (no extra roles/software)

2) Network Design & VLAN Segmentation



Design and implement VLANs (IDs + subnets), including:



Servers VLAN

Office PCs VLAN

Voice VLAN (IP Phones)

Staff Wi-Fi VLAN

Guest Wi-Fi VLAN (internet only)

CCTV VLAN

Management VLAN (for devices)

(Optional) Backup VLAN



Provide:



IP addressing scheme

DHCP scopes (via Sophos or Windows—state which and why)

3) Switch Configuration (Core + Room Switches)

Configure:

Trunk ports (core ? room switches, AP uplinks, Hyper-V host)

Access ports (PCs, printers, cameras)

Implement:

VLAN tagging

Voice VLAN for Grandstream phones

STP (basic loop protection)

Document:

Port map (what connects where)

4) Sophos Firewall Configuration

Configure WAN (fiber) and LAN interfaces



Implement inter-VLAN routing and rules:



Allow



Staff LAN/Wi-Fi ? Servers (required ports only)

Voice VLAN ? PBX/required services

CCTV VLAN ? NVR/monitoring only



Deny



Guest ? all internal VLANs

CCTV ? other VLANs (except required)

General access ? Management VLAN

Configure:

NAT

Basic security hardening

Optional VPN (if needed)

5) Hyper-V Networking (R630 + Huawei Hosts)

Configure External Virtual Switch(es)

D
 and implement:

VLAN tagging at vNIC level

(Optional) NIC Teaming / SET on R630 (state reasoning)

Ensure:

VMs are placed in correct VLANs (Servers / Dev / etc.)

No exposure of management interfaces to user VLANs

6) Wi-Fi Deployment

Configure 3 SSIDs:

Staff ? mapped to Staff VLAN

Guest ? mapped to Guest VLAN (internet only)

(Optional) Management

Configure:

WPA2/WPA3 security

VLAN tagging on AP uplinks (trunk)

Provide:

AP placement recommendation (based on office layout)

7) VoIP (Grandstream Phones)

Configure:

Voice VLAN

QoS (basic prioritization for voice)

Ensure:

PC pass-through ports work correctly (data VLAN)

Verify:

Phones obtain correct VLAN and IP

8) CCTV Network

Place cameras in CCTV VLAN

Ensure:

Isolated from user networks

Accessible only from authorized devices (e.g., NVR/management)

9) Backup Architecture (Critical)



Design correct Hyper-V backup approach:



Must include:



VM-aware backup (VSS)

Backup target: NAS (primary)

Optional: secondary copy to cloud (e.g., iDrive)

Clearly state:

Recommended software (e.g., Veeam or equivalent)

Backup schedule (daily incremental)

Retention policy

Restore testing procedure

10) Documentation (Mandatory Deliverable)



Provide a concise but complete document including:



Network diagram (logical + physical)

VLAN table (IDs, subnets, purpose)

Switch port mapping

Firewall rule summary

AD structure (OU, roles)

Backup design and procedures

Credentials/hand-over notes (securely shared)

Deliverables



At project completion:



Fully configured and working network



Domain Controllers operational and replicating

VLANs and firewall rules implemented

Wi-Fi (staff + guest) working correctly

VoIP and CCTV functioning within correct VLANs

Backup system configured and tested

Complete documentation provided



Acceptance Criteria

Users can log in via AD from wired and Wi-Fi networks

Guest Wi-Fi has no access to internal resources

Phones operate correctly with voice VLAN

Cameras are isolated and accessible only as intended

Backup completes successfully and test restore works

No management interfaces exposed to general users



Engagement Model

One-time fixed project

Remote execution (with scheduled sessions if required)

Engineer to provide estimated hours and timeline upfront





I prefer to proceed on a fixed project basis. Based on the scope, please propose a fixed price and timeline. I am expecting structured delivery with milestones (design, implementation, testing, documentation).

title: Video Editor - DTC
description:
AI-Focused Video Editor – Paid Ads (DTC Apparel Brand)

Location: Remote

Type: Full-Time

Industry: DTC E-commerce / Apparel



What We're Looking For

We're looking for an AI-Focused Video Editor with strong experience creating high-performing paid ads for DTC brands — and a genuine command of modern AI tools to produce scroll-stopping creatives at scale.

Our brand grows through paid social, and the videos we produce must capture attention instantly and drive conversions. This is not a traditional editing role. We expect you to use AI tools as a core part of your workflow — from generating visuals and voiceovers to creating realistic AI-presenter ads — while maintaining a high creative bar throughout.

You'll work closely with our marketing, creative, and growth teams producing video creatives across Meta, TikTok, and other paid platforms.

This role is a great fit if you've worked with DTC brands in:



Fashion or apparel

Lingerie / underwear

Female-focused brands





Your Role

AI-Powered Ad Creation

You'll be expected to produce high-quality, realistic ads using AI tools — including AI avatar and synthetic presenter formats, AI-generated footage and imagery, and AI voiceovers. The output must look and feel professional and native to the platform. AI-generated content that looks cheap or robotic is not the standard we're after.

Paid Social Ads

Edit short-form video creatives for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and Meta Ads. You understand strong hooks, retention-driven pacing, and what makes an ad actually convert — not just look good.

UGC-Style Editing

Edit UGC-style ads that feel authentic and platform-native — testimonials, product demos, problem/solution formats, and lifestyle content. You know how to make content feel real, whether it was filmed or AI-generated.

Creative Iteration

Produce multiple variations of each concept for testing — different hooks, pacing, formats, and openings. Speed matters. AI tools should allow you to move faster and test more, not slow things down.

Trend Awareness

Stay on top of what's performing on TikTok and paid social, and bring those formats into our ad creatives.



What We Require



Proven experience editing paid ads for DTC brands

Hands-on experience creating AI avatar / synthetic presenter ads

Ability to produce realistic AI-generated video and imagery

Experience with AI voiceover tools integrated into finished creatives

Strong understanding of hooks, pacing, and conversion-driven editing

Experience with UGC-style content

Familiarity with standard editing software (Premiere, CapCut, Final Cut, etc.)



Strong preference for candidates with experience in fashion, apparel, or female-focused DTC brands.



What We Offer



Full-time remote position

Competitive salary + performance-based bonuses

A fast-moving, creatively ambitious team

Long-term growth opportunity as AI video continues to evolve





How To Apply

Please include:



CV

Portfolio — especially any AI-assisted or AI-generated ad creatives

Examples of paid social ads and UGC-style content

The AI tools you work with and how you use them

DTC brands you've worked with

A short note on what you'd bring to our brand



To confirm you read the full posting, include the word "AI PRO" at the end of your application. Applications without this will not be reviewed.

title: Product Researcher
description:
About Us



We are a fast-growing Scandinavian direct-response e-commerce brand selling health, wellness, beauty, and supplement products. We operate on a performance-first model — we test fast, scale what works, and cut what doesn’t. Our traffic runs primarily through Meta 
/Instagram) and we are always looking for the next winning product to add to our catalogue.



We’re a lean, fully remote team. Every role we hire is built to scale with the business. If you want to work at the front end of product discovery in direct-response e-commerce — finding the products before anyone else does — this is the role for you.



The Role



We are hiring a full-time Product Researcher to keep our product testing pipeline stocked with qualified, proven opportunities. Your job is to find 5–10 winning product candidates per week that pass our qualification criteria, log them in our shared Google Sheet, and deliver them on time every Monday.



This is not a creative role. It is a research and filtering role. You are the first gate in our product selection process — the quality of what you find directly determines what we test and ultimately what we scale.



Output expectation



5–10 fully qualified product leads per week, logged in the Google Sheet with all required data fields completed. Quantity without quality does not count — every lead must pass all qualification criteria before it is logged.



Responsibilities



Research and identify 5–10 winning product candidates per week across health, beauty, wellness, and high-pain-angle viral niches

Qualify every product against our criteria before logging — do not submit products that do not pass all thresholds

Log each qualified product in the shared Google Sheet with all required data fields completed

Use TrendTrack, Meta Ad Library, and any other free tools available to validate demand and competitor activity

Deliver the completed weekly product sheet every Monday before 10:00 AM

Flag standout opportunities — products with unusually strong signals — immediately without waiting for Monday delivery

Stay current on trending products in health, beauty, wellness and viral product categories



Qualifications & Skills



Proven experience in product research for e-commerce or dropshipping — you know what a winning product looks like (Or show you’re competent)

Familiarity with Meta Ad Library, TrendTrack, AliExpress and similar research tools

Strong understanding of what makes a product work in paid social: pain angle, mass appeal, visual demonstration, impulse buy potential

Ability to filter ruthlessly — you do not submit weak products just to hit a number

Detail-oriented: every field in the sheet must be filled in correctly, every time

Self-managed: you hit your Monday deadline without being chased

Previous experience working for dropshipping or direct-response e-commerce brands is a strong plus



Who You Are



You do what you say. You finish what you start. Deadlines are not suggestions.

You have an obsessive attention to detail. Nothing gets past you — and we mean nothing. (Seriously: include “the devil is in the details” as your answer to the first application question. If you skip this, your application will not be reviewed.)

You think like a business owner. You understand that the products you find become the products we bet money on. You take that seriously.

You are results-driven. You want to find winners, not just fill a sheet.



The product bonus window is 60 days from the date of the product’s first live Meta campaign. Team bonuses apply to all team members based on total monthly order volume across the entire business and are paid automatically when a bracket is reached.



Earnings potential

A strong month — 3 products tested, 1 hitting 50,000 SEK, business at 2,000 orders — means $200 base + $50 product bonus + $20 team bonus = $270. As the business scales to 5,000+ monthly orders, team bonuses add $50 on top every month automatically.



Trial Task & Period



Before the paid trial begins, we run a short unpaid trial task:



We give you a product category

Within 48 hours you send 3 product leads that pass all qualification criteria

We review quality, format, and filtering ability

If the quality is there, we move to a paid one-month trial at $200/month



After the one-month paid trial we evaluate together and move to the full ongoing arrangement if both parties are happy. The trial protects you as much as it protects us.



How to Apply



Send your application with the following:



Answer to the first question (you know what it is)

Brief description of your product research experience and tools you use

1–2 examples of products you have researched and why you found them promising

Your availability and expected start date



Applications without the attention test answer or without relevant experience will not be reviewed.

title: Sales Development Representative (SDR) – High-Volume Cold Calling (B2G)
description:
Summary

Remote / Work From Home | Full Time | Company based in California



About Us



Bi-Rite Global is a certified small business and a trusted retailer that supplies a wide range of products to government agencies — including federal, state, and education sectors. Based in California, we are rapidly expanding and focused on delivering quality products, competitive pricing, and reliable service to our government partners.



Role Summary

We are looking for a Sales Development Representative (SDR) – High-Volume Cold Calling (B2G)  that is responsible for initiating outbound calls to federal, state, and local government agencies to identify contracting opportunities, introduce company services, and qualify decision-makers involved in procurement. The role supports the business development pipeline by generating leads, setting capability briefings, and gathering intelligence on upcoming bids and purchasing needs. This role offers a full time employment and the opportunity to grow beyond a purely SDR function into a full sales role, if that’s something you’re interested in.



Key Responsibilities

• Conduct high-volume outbound calls to government agency front desks and effectively navigate phone trees to reach relevant departments

• Ask targeted, strategic questions to identify key procurement or purchasing decision-makers (by name and role)

• Introduce company capabilities and differentiate services

• Qualify opportunities based on agency needs, budgets, and timelines

• Schedule capability briefings or discovery meetings for sales/business development teams

• Gather information on upcoming solicitations, RFPs, and purchasing cycles

• Accurately capture and log contact details (name, title,
, direct phone line) into a CRM or tracking spreadsheet

• Professionally handle gatekeepers and objections, including situations where information is not readily disclosed

• Maintain organized and up-to-date contact lists to support ongoing outreach efforts

• Track targets such as contacts reached, meetings set, and opportunities generated



Role Focus

• This position is primarily focused on contact discovery and list building, not full-cycle sales or closing.



Qualifications

• Professional phone presence and clear communication skills

• Persistent and comfortable with high-volume outbound calling and rejection

• Strong research skills to identify government decision-makers

• Organized with accurate CRM documentation and time management

• Understanding of B2G (Business to Government) sales or ability to learn quickly

• Experience in lead generation, appointment setting, telemarketing, or inside sales

• Exposure to government procurement or public-sector contracting preferred

• Familiarity with RFP/RFQ processes, vendor registrations, and contract vehicles is an advantage

• Able to clearly present company capabilities and services



Work Environment

• 100% Remote (must have reliable work-from-home setup)

• Structured schedule aligned with business hours of government agencies

• Use of approved scripts and compliance-focused communication

• Calls primarily to public offices, vendors, or registered partners

• Performance tracked by call quality, accuracy, and documentation — not aggressive contracting

• Collaboration with compliance, procurement, and contracts teams

• Strict adherence to data privacy, confidentiality, and record-keeping standards

• Professional and formal communication style required

• Write "I want this job" on the
 subject line.



Technical Requirements

• Stable internet (minimum 50 Mbps download/upload on fiber connection)

• Backup internet (LTE mobile data or pocket Wi-Fi)

• Laptop or desktop with Intel i5 equivalent or higher, 8GB RAM

• Quality headset with noise-canceling microphone

• Functional webcam



What’s Next

1. Interested candidates should submit a face-to-camera, 2-minute introduction Loom video explaining why you’re the ideal fit for this role.



2. Attach your Loom URL, resume, and any relevant experience samples and send it including your complete name in our message inbox.

title: Full-Time Social Media Marketing Expert for Tucson Home Services Brand
description:
About Us



Ultra Men Property Services is a local home and property services company based in Tucson, Arizona. We help homeowners and businesses keep their properties clean, protected, and looking their best.



Our services include solar panel cleaning, bird proofing, yard cleanup, junk removal, and other property maintenance services. We are a hardworking, reliable, results-driven company that takes pride in doing quality work for our customers.



You can view our Instagram here:





About the Role



We are looking for a full-time Social Media Marketing Expert / Instagram Growth Specialist to help us grow our online presence and generate more local leads.



This role is not just about making nice graphics. We need someone who understands how to take real job-site photos and videos, turn them into engaging content, and use social media to help bring in more customers.



You will manage and grow our Instagram and
 presence, create reels and posts, write captions, schedule content, track performance, and help us build a stronger local brand in Tucson.



The right person is creative, organized, consistent, and understands how to market a local service business.



Responsibilities



Your main responsibilities will include:



* Create Instagram Reels, posts, stories, and
 content

* Edit raw job-site videos and before/after photos into engaging content

* Write captions that sound natural, local, and conversion-focused

* Research hashtags, content ideas, and local Tucson marketing opportunities

* Schedule posts consistently across social platforms

* Track basic analytics and suggest improvements

* Repurpose content across Instagram,
, TikTok, and Google Business Profile when applicable

* Help create simple promotional campaigns for solar panel cleaning, bird proofing, yard cleanup, and junk removal

* Suggest content ideas based on competitors and local home-service trends

* Build and maintain a weekly/monthly content calendar

* Coordinate with the owner/team to request photos, videos, customer reviews, and job updates

* Look for ways to turn social media attention into real customer inquiries and booked jobs



Requirements



We are looking for someone who has:



* Strong English writing skills

* Experience managing Instagram and
 for businesses

* Experience creating short-form video content

* Experience using Canva, CapCut, Meta Business Suite, or similar tools

* Ability to edit reels, short videos, before/after photos, and simple graphics

* Understanding of local service business marketing

* Good communication skills

* Strong organization and consistency

* Ability to work independently without being micromanaged

* A creative mindset and willingness to suggest new ideas



Bonus experience:



* Home services marketing

* Contractor, cleaning, landscaping, solar, junk removal, or property service businesses

* Lead generation through social media

* TikTok content creation

* Google Business Profile posting

* Basic paid social media ads



Work Schedule



This is a full-time position.



We are looking for someone available 40 hours per week. Please include your preferred working hours in your application.



Some overlap with Arizona business hours is preferred so we can communicate, review content, plan campaigns, and post timely updates when needed.



Compensation



Pay will depend on your experience and skill level.



Please include your expected monthly salary in your application.



How to Apply



To apply, please send the following:



1. A brief introduction about yourself

2. Links to social media accounts you have managed

3. 2–3 examples of reels, posts, captions, graphics, or campaigns you created

4. A short explanation of how you would 

5. Your expected monthly salary

6. Your availability and preferred working hours

7. A list of the tools you use for content creation, scheduling, and reporting



Screening Question



To show that you read the full job post, start your application with the words:



Ultra Tucson Growth



Applications that do not include this phrase may not be reviewed.



Who We Are Looking For



We want someone who can think strategically, not just create pretty graphics.



The ideal candidate understands that social media should help build trust, show real work, attract local customers, and generate leads. You should be able to look at raw photos and videos from job sites and turn them into content that makes people want to contact us.



If you are creative, dependable, organized, and excited to help grow a local service business, we would love to hear from you.

title: B2B Growth Specialist / Cold Caller
description:
## **Job Title: B2B Growth Specialist / Cold Caller**

**Company:** Pylon Partners



Pylon Partners is looking for a relentless "Hunter" to onboard businesses into our ecosystem. We specialize in providing Automated Lead Capture and Nurture systems for the Home Care and Home Services industries. 



We provide the tech, the scripts, and the leads—you provide the voice that closes the gap between a business owner’s problem and our solution. This is a purely performance-based role designed for someone who wants to build a massive **residual income** stream.



---



### **The Value Proposition**

You aren't just selling a service; you are selling **time and growth.** You will be calling business owners in the Home Care and Home Services sectors (HVAC, Roofing, Plumbing, etc.) to show them how Pylon Partners automates their lead capture and follow-up so they never miss a customer again.



---



### **Job Responsibilities**

* **Outbound Prospecting:** Execute high-volume cold calls to business owners and decision-makers in the home services and home care sectors.

* **Lead Qualification:** Identify pain points in a business's current lead handling and present our automation as the definitive solution.

* **Onboarding & Closing:** Manage the full sales cycle from the initial cold call to onboarding the business into the Pylon Partners system.

* **Pipeline Management:** Maintain an organized follow-up system to ensure no potential partner falls through the cracks.

* **Relationship Retention:** Act as the primary point of contact for your onboarded businesses to ensure they remain active (protecting your monthly residual).



---



### **The Compensation: Uncapped Residual Growth**

We pay for the **lifetime value** of the client. As long as the business you onboard stays with Pylon Partners, you get paid.



* **$300 USD Monthly Residual:** For every business you onboard, you receive $300 every single month they remain a client.

* **$1,000 USD Stackable Base:** For every 5 active clients you maintain in a month, you unlock a $1,000 USD base pay.



**Potential Earnings:**

* **5 Active Businesses:** $1,500 (Residuals) + $1,000 (Base) = **$2,500/mo**

* **10 Active Businesses:** $3,000 (Residuals) + $2,000 (Base) = **$5,000/mo**

* **20 Active Businesses:** $6,000 (Residuals) + $4,000 (Base) = **$10,000/mo**



---



### **What We Provide**

We handle the technical heavy lifting so you can focus on the conversation.

* **Proven Systems:** You are selling an automated lead nurture engine that works.

* **Full Sales Kit:** Access to battle-tested B2B scripts, strategies, and phone lists.

* **Total Autonomy:** No set hours. If you deliver results, you manage your own day.

* **Loyalty:** If you take care of the growth, Pylon Partners will take care of you with the best care possible.



---



### **The Requirements & Performance Clause**

* **B2B Grit:** You are comfortable speaking to business owners and handling objections.

* **Output-Driven:** You understand that **no output = no pay.**

* **The 30-Day Rule:** We move fast. **If one month passes and zero businesses are onboarded, you will be laid off immediately.**



---



### **How to Apply**

To be considered, you **must** submit:

1. **Resume.**

2. **Cover Letter.**

3. **1-Minute Voice Recording:** Explain your past experience in B2B cold calling and why you’re the right person to represent Pylon Partners.

title: Ecommerce Executive (Shopee, Tiktok, Lazada Manage Seller Center)
description:
Join our team and become part of a dynamic, global organization of 40 professionals across Korea, Singapore, Malaysia, and the Philippines. Collaborate closely with our regional teams to grow an exciting portfolio of leading Korean brands, including Beauty of Joseon, Needly, Tirtir, and other top-trending names in the market.



We are seeking a confident and experienced tea
mber who can independently oversee e-commerce operations, take proactive initiative, and consistently deliver results with minimal supervision, ensuring long-term success and stability in the role.



Key Responsibilities:



- Oversee day-to-end e-commerce operations (catalog, price checks, campaign execution, inventory management, shipping, logistics, customer service and returns) for the Seller Center Stores and the international House of Hur accounts across Singapore, Malaysia, Taiwan, and Vietnam.

- Manage product listings across multiple online platforms (Shopee, Lazada, TikTok Shop), ensuring accuracy, quality, and completeness (basic photo editing and product research).

- Monitor promo pricing set ups, pricing strategies and campaign promotions (e.g., 11.11, 12.12, Payday Sales) to drive sales growth performance.

- Track and analyze sales data, competitor activity, inventory levels, and shipment status, preparing actionable performance reports.

- Confidently present weekly and monthly data to management, business analytics metrics into clear strategies.

- Maintain overall e-commerce account health, coordinating with teams to resolve customer service issues, refunds, returns, and fraud cases.

- Collaborate with team and provide ad-hoc support for special events and projects.



Qualifications:



- 2–4 years of hands-on experience managing e-commerce operations on Shopee, Lazada, TikTok Seller Centre or Bigseller Account, or similar is required.

- Strong understanding of online campaign management, pricing strategies, and platform algorithms.

- Proficient in Google sheets and Excel. Managing large datasets, utilizing advanced formulas (e.g., VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables), and generating performance reports.

- Highly organized, able to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

- Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively in multicultural teams.

- Seeking a dedicated full-time and long term role.





Benefits:

- Stable full time with leave benefits, up to 14 days annual leave, holiday leave, birthday leave.

- Opportunity to be part of a growing team and Korean Beauty Brands.

- Travel Opportunity to Singapore during events.

- Comfortable work environment, same working time zone as the Philippines.

- Structured salary increase and promotion based on performance.



To apply, please answer a few short pre-screening questions via the Google Form linked below. This helps us better understand your background and fit for the role before the interview stage.

title: Part-Time Video Editor Wanted — Facebook & Instagram Ads | eCommerce | Long-Term
description:
ABOUT THIS ROLE

I run a direct-to-consumer footwear brand selling to women 50+ in the US via
 and Instagram ads. Video creative is the core driver of our growth — and I need an editor who genuinely understands direct response, not just someone who can cut clips together.

This is daily, ongoing work. If you're the right person, this becomes a stable long-term contract.



SCOPE OF WORK

Every day I provide 3 scripts, raw UGC footage, and B-roll. You deliver 3 NET new video ads per day (3 scripts × 3 hook variants each so 9 video's total), including captions, text overlays, and correct pacing for Meta placements. You'll also source supplementary footage where needed (or create with AI). I handle scripting. You handle everything on the edit side. Clean handoff, clear expectations, daily rhythm.



YOU'RE A STRONG FIT IF

— You've edited video ads for eCommerce brands on Meta

— You understand hook structure, scroll-stopping openers, and DR pacing

— You can source organic-looking footage that doesn't feel like stock

— Your edits feel native to a
 feed — not like a TV commercial

— You can handle daily delivery without constant follow-up

— You have a real portfolio of Meta eCommerce ad edits to show



TO APPLY

Skip the generic cover letter. Answer these four questions directly in your proposal:



1. What's the most important element of a video ad's first 3 seconds — and why?



2. Describe the last eCommerce video ad you edited that actually performed well. What made it work?



3. Walk me through how you approach editing 3 hook variants from a single script. What changes between each variant?



4. What's the most common mistake video editors make when editing ads for a 50+ audience?



Also include a Google Drive link or direct links to at least 5 Meta-style eCommerce video ads you've edited. 



Strong applicants move to a short video call. Top candidates receive a test edit.

title: Phone Client Communication Specialist (Property Management, WFH)

_____________________



MINDSET REQUIREMENTS

• You take ownership - you treat the business like it’s yours

• You go the extra mile without being asked

• You are reliable, consistent, and professional every day

• You want long-term, possibly lifetime, stability - not job hopping every few years

_____________________



ABOUT US

• Small and close like family

• We value long-term tea
mbers who want stability and consistency

FINAL NOTE

Wants to stay and grow long-term

title: Sales & Outreach VA
description:
About Us

Outframe is a B2B product design studio based in Europe. We work with established SaaS companies — fintech, supply chain, CRM, enterprise software — helping them modernise their software designs. We have a small senior team, a strong track record, and a good reputation in our niche. Our last outreach VA worked with us for 3 years.



What You'll Do

Split roughly between two types of work:



Job board monitoring & outreach:

• Monitor Dribbble, Wellfound, LinkedIn Jobs, and a few other boards for B2B design contract postings

• Qualify postings against our criteria and apply using provided templates — adapting them per posting

• We're building scrapers to automate parts of this, so you'll help set up and manage those workflows over time



LinkedIn prospecting:

• Build targeted lists of founders, CPOs, and product leaders at B2B SaaS companies using Sales Navigator

• Send personalised outreach and manage follow-ups — not copy-paste blasts

• Track everything in Airtable and flag warm leads for handoff



You're a Fit If

• You have some experience with LinkedIn Sales Navigator and outreach — you know the basics

• You're comfortable doing repetitive monitoring work alongside more strategic prospecting

• Your written English is perfect — outreach needs to sound human, not templated

• You're self-managed, reliable, and don't need daily check-ins

• You've worked in sales support, BDR, or agency-side outreach before — even if junior



Not Required

• You don't need a design background

• No sales calls or client-facing work

• No fixed hours — fully async via Slack



The Deal

• Start part-time (20 hrs/week) with a clear path to full-time within 3–6 months if it's working

• Long-term role — our last VA worked with us for 3 years

• Real input into the outreach strategy — we want your recommendations on what's working, what to test, and how to improve the approach over time

• You'll have templates, ICPs, and messaging frameworks ready — not starting from scratch



How to Apply

Send a short message (not a template) with:

• What outreach or prospecting tools you've used

• One example of outreach work you've done — what you targeted, what you sent, what happened

• Why this role fits where you are right now.
