title: Video Editor (PHP 358/HR - USD $6/HR | PART Time)
description:
Hey!



We are looking for a Video Editor ROCKSTAR who will turn our long form content into high-performing short form clips and weekly YouTube videos.



We are a marketing and education company that works with English speakers and already have a team of high-performing individuals from the Philippines. Mastery of the English language is a requirement and will be tested prior to hiring.



BENEFITS INCLUDE:

- 100% remote

- Weekly pay

- USD $6 / Hour = 350 PHP / Hour

- Paid training

- No work during the weekends

- Opportunity to go from part time to full time

- We pay for the AI and creative tools you will use (Adobe, Descript, Opus Clip, Canva Pro)

- Grow with a team that values initiative, speed, and high standards



WHAT YOU WILL DO:



Short form (main focus):

- Take long form content (webinars, trainings, YouTube videos, podcasts) and turn it into short form clips for Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and LinkedIn

- Add attention-grabbing hooks, captions, emojis, B-roll, overlays, and pattern interrupts

- Create short testimonial videos from coaching calls, client interviews, and other raw footage

- Organize and manage video assets so clips are easy to find and reuse



Long form & channel support:

- Edit 1 piece of long form content per week (highlight key 
nts, add B-roll, overlays, and graphics)

- Publish that long form video on YouTube (upload, title, description, tags, end screens, cards)

- Create and upload custom YouTube thumbnails that drive clicks

- Handle one-off editing requests as needed (simple promos, clips, intros/outros, etc.)

- Analyze performance of published videos in YouTube Studio (CTR, views, watch time, retention) and provide simple reports and recommendations



Collaboration & cadence:

- Meet once a week with the owner of the company for feedback, direction, and priorities

- Join 1 monthly content planning session with the team

- Use AI tools (that we pay for) to speed up editing, transcription, captioning, and repurposing while still polishing by hand



Expected volume (part-time ~20 hours per week):

- 20–30 finished short form clips per week (30–90 seconds each)

- 1 edited and published long form YouTube video per week

- Ongoing testimonial edits and one-off requests as needed



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:



Experience & portfolio:

- Proven experience editing short form content from long form videos

- Strong portfolio showing:

Before/after edits from long form to short form

YouTube videos you have edited and thumbnails you have designed

Short testimonial / social proof videos (a plus)



Tool competence (we provide licenses):

You will work daily inside:

- Adobe Premiere Pro (primary editor)

- Basic Adobe After Effects (simple animations, lower thirds, transitions)

- Descript (transcription, rough cuts, repurposing)

- Opus Clip (AI clipping & short-form ideas)

- Canva Pro (YouTube thumbnails & simple graphics)

- YouTube Studio (publishing & analytics)

- Google Drive, Google Docs/Sheets (file management, tracking)

- ClickUp (task management)

- Slack (communication)



You don’t need to be a master of every tool on day one, but you must already be strong in at least one pro editor (Premiere / Final Cut / DaVinci) and be able to learn the rest quickly.

Having experience with video editing is a requirement to be considered for this role.

We will ask you what your favorite dog breed is, answer chowchow in your application so we know you have read the full job posting.

We will start with relatively limited hours (around 20 hours per week to begin) with the opportunity for it to grow as our relationship develops.



REQUIREMENTS:

- Mastery of the English language (spoken and written)

- Strong project management skills and ability to hit volume targets consistently

- Comfortable reviewing analytics and making basic performance recommendations

- Quiet environment to work



TECH REQUIREMENTS (MINIMUM):

- Computer: Windows 10/11 PC or Mac (released within the last 4–5 years)

CPU: Intel i7 / Ryzen 7 / Apple M1 or better (or equivalent)

RAM: Minimum 16 GB (32 GB strongly preferred)

Storage: At least 512 GB SSD, with 150+ GB free for active projects

GPU: Dedicated graphics card with 4 GB VRAM or more (e.g., NVIDIA GTX 1660 / RTX series or equivalent). For Mac, M1/M2 with integrated GPU is fine.



- Software capability:

Must be able to run Adobe Premiere Pro / After Effects smoothly while having multiple Chrome tabs, cloud drives, and AI tools (Descript / Opus Clip / etc.) open.



-High Speed Internet: Stable enough for uploading/downloading large video files and live calls without interruption

Reliable backup option (mobile data or secondary ISP) is a plus



- Other:

Quality headphones and microphone for edit reviews and team calls

Quiet, dedicated workspace suitable for focused editing work



If your current setup struggles with Adobe Premiere or AI tools like Descript / Opus Clip, please upgrade first before applying. This role requires fast, reliable hardware to hit our weekly volume targets.



SCHEDULE:

You must be available to work 4 hours per day, following this schedule: 3 PM – 7 PM Philippines time.



You must APPLY with this link:

title: Video Ad Editor For Supplement
description:
Video Editor for AI UGC Meta Ads

Remote

We're a 6-figure DTC supplement brand running AI 
 ads on
 and Instagram. We need an editor who can take a script and turn it into a scroll-stopping ad — generating the AI UGC, voiceovers, and b-roll from scratch using our SOP and tools, then editing it all into a finished ad that converts.

If you're not experienced, don't apply. We're not training anyone.



What You'll Be Doing



Editing short-form AI UGC ads and long and short storyline VSLs for Meta (mainly 9:16)

Working with AI avatar footage (Capcut, Higgsfield, Flow, ElevenLabs, etc.) and syncing it cleanly with AI voiceovers

Sourcing and layering b-roll, product shots, and supporting visuals

Following our scripts and timing direction beat-by-beat

Adding text overlays, captions, hooks, and CTAs

Light sound design and music selection

Delivering polished, on-brand ads with minimal revisions



What You'll Receive From Us



A finished script for every ad

A detailed SOP walking you through our exact production process

Access to all the AI tools you'll need (avatar generation, voiceover, etc.)

Brand guidelines and style references from our top-performing ads



You're handling the full production from script to final cut. We're handing you the system — your job is to execute it cleanly and consistently.

What You Need



Proven e-commerce / DTC ad editing experience (not vlogs, weddings, or music videos)

Experience producing both long and short storyline VSLs for ads

Hands-on experience with AI UGC tools — (Capcut, Higgsfield, Flow, ElevenLabs, etc.)

Comfortable generating AI avatars and voiceovers from scratch, not just editing pre-made footage

Comfortable sourcing your own b-roll to support the script

Comfortable cleaning up AI avatar quirks (lip-sync drift, awkward pauses, robotic pacing) so the final product feels human

Strong ability to follow a documented SOP without constant hand-holding

Premiere Pro, CapCut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve proficiency

24–48 hour turnaround, consistent quality



Bonus Points



Experience with ElevenLabs, Higgsfield, Flow, Kling, and other AI generation tools

Ability to spot when an AI generation needs to be regenerated vs. salvaged

A portfolio of supplement or health/wellness ads that have actually run (with results, if you have them)





Expectations



Send your resume with your application. No resume = no reply.

You must have DTC / e-commerce ad experience. Not vlogs or music videos.

You must have actually produced AI UGC ads and long + short VSLs before — not just edited around AI footage someone else generated. Show us examples.

You must complete a paid trial before hiring. We send you a script, the SOP, and tool access. You produce one full ad end-to-end. Takes about 1–2 hours. Lets both of us see if it's a fit.



Standards

Our ads compete against the best in DTC supplements. If your AI UGC looks stiff, robotic, or sloppy — bad lip-sync, awkward cuts, dead pacing, or off-brand visuals — this isn't the role. If you can take a script and an SOP and produce ads that feel native to the feed and convert, you're exactly who we want.

Again — if you don't have direct experience producing AI UGC ads and VSLs, do not apply.

How to Apply



Resume with links to AI UGC ads and storyline VSLs (long and short) you've produced (required — generic e-commerce ads aren't enough)

Which AI UGC tools you've worked with most

Timezone and weekly availability

One line on what makes your AI UGC ads convert



No resume + no AI UGC portfolio = no interview.

title: Brand & UI Designer
description:
I for Design Studio is looking for a talented Senior Brand & UI Designer to join the studio on an ongoing basis. This is a real, long-term opportunity. If you're the right person, I want to build something together.



___



A little about us



I for Design Studio is a boutique brand and website studio working with established business owners who have outgrown their online presence. Think solicitors, interior designers, accountants, and allied health professionals. People who are genuinely good at what they do, have built real credibility, and are ready for a brand and website that finally reflects it.



We're a small, collaborative team led by myself (founder and creative director). We're honest with each other, serious about doing great work, and completely allergic to ego and agency burnout culture.



Design style matters here, a lot. We create work that's refined, intentional, and personality-led. Warm, considered, and commercial rather than trend-chasing. If that's the kind of work you gravitate toward naturally, we'll get along well.



___



What the work looks like:

• Designing brand identities and visual systems

• Creating brand guidelines and client-ready documentation

• Designing website UI in Figma

• Supporting website builds (Squarespace experience is a bonus, not essential)

• Designing marketing collateral, social content, and digital assets

• Communicating with clients clearly and professionally when needed



___



What I'm looking for:

• 3+ years experience in brand and UI design (flexible if the work is exceptional)

• Strong skills in Adobe Creative Suite and Figma

• Someone who can genuinely adapt to a brief — our clients range from wedding florists to law firms, and you'll need to serve each aesthetic with equal care

• Confident working independently and owning projects end to end

• Proactive, organised, and deadline-driven. Reliability is everything here

• A clear, open communicator who flags issues early rather than going quiet

• Someone who thrives under creative direction rather than needing to lead it



___



Good to know:

• This is a long-term ongoing role with real room to grow

• Fully remote, flexible hours with overlap required during AEST business hours

• A short paid test brief is part of the process — we want to see how you work, not just what you've made



___



How to apply:

If this sounds like your kind of work and your portfolio speaks for itself,
 
 with:

• Subject line: I'm your next Brand & UI Designer

• A short introduction about you, your experience, and why this feels like a good fit

• Your CV and portfolio or website link (essential)

• Your ideal hourly rate in AUD



Please note: Due to the volume of applications, I'll only be in touch if your work and experience are a strong fit.

title: Growth Marketing Assistant -  Video + Content Wizard!
description:
3dimages.ai helps e-commerce brands replace flat photography with photorealistic 3D Models, renders and AR-ready assets. It's a fast-growing space, and we're building the content engine to match.



We're hiring a Growth Marketing Assistant to work directly with co-founder Ganesh and own the execution side of our marketing - newsletters, SEO content, LinkedIn, video, and
.



What the role looks like:

You'll use AI tools daily (Claude and others) to produce content at genuine scale - not one post a week, but a full, systematic content machine. That means writing sharp LinkedIn copy, crafting compelling
 newsletters on Beehive, building SEO blog workflows, and helping automate the systems that keep it all moving.



Video is core to this role.... You'll be clipping long-form content into short, high-retention hooks, spotting the 
nts that stop the scroll, and packaging them for maximum reach. You understand pacing, pattern interrupts, and strong opens — and you know how to engineer that consistently, not just occasionally.



Writing is equally important. You understand what makes a hook land - the first line that stops someone mid-scroll, the framing that makes someone feel like they'd be stupid not to keep reading. 



You know how to open with tension, build with curiosity, and close with something worth sharing. Whether it's a LinkedIn post, a newsletter, or a caption, you write with a clear point of view and you make it count.





You'll thrive here if you



• Use AI as a craft, not a shortcut

• Write hooks that actually hook — and know why they work

• Have a sharp eye for what makes video content go viral

• Can clip and edit video fast, without hand-holding

• Are fluent across LinkedIn,
, and short-form video

• Like building systems as much as executing them



Why apply

Direct founder access, real ownership, fast-moving space. If you want to learn what AI-native growth marketing actually looks like in practice, this is it.

title: photo editor
description:
Hi there,



My name is Maxime Bernadin, and I’m a wedding photographer based in the South of France, working mainly across Provence and Paris.



I’m currently looking for a talented and reliable photo editor to support me with my wedding and session edits this season — and ideally to build a long-term collaboration over the coming years.



For this year, I have around 15–20 weddings booked, along with a few couple sessions throughout the season. Most of my work takes place between May and October.



I work with a refined, natural, and timeless aesthetic, with a strong focus on light, tones, and consistency. My goal is always to deliver a clean, elegant, and high-end final gallery.



To streamline the process, I use an AI-based tool to create a solid base edit. This means you would receive a Lightroom catalog where the images are already partially edited, allowing you to focus on refining and elevating the final result.



What I need help with:

Editing approximately 600–900 images per wedding in Lightroom

Editing approximately 100–150 images per couple session

Fine-tuning exposure, white balance, and tones

Cropping, straightening, and ensuring consistency across the gallery

Subtle dodging & burning when needed to enhance depth and light

Remove unaesthetic objects



Making sure the final result aligns with my visual identity (you will be guided through my process)

Important to know:



Because the base edit is already applied, the work is focused on refinement rather than full editing from scratch.



I’m currently looking for someone for €0.15 per image, depending on experience and quality.



What I’m NOT looking for:

Someone who struggles with deadlines or organization

Someone who cannot take feedback or adapt quickly

Inconsistent or rushed work



What I’m looking for:

Detail-oriented and consistent in their work

Reliable, organized, and respectful of deadlines

A good eye for light, color, and natural skin tones

Someone who understands a high-end / luxury aesthetic

Someone who enjoys working on emotional, storytelling-driven wedding imagery

Fluent in English (French is a plus, but not required)



Ideally, I’m looking for someone who can deliver:



Weddings within 5–7 business days

Sessions within 2–3 business days



If this sounds like you, I’d love to hear from you.



Please include:



Your

A few words about your experience in photo editing

Examples of work you’ve edited (before/after if possible)

Your rate per image

And a short note on why you enjoy editing wedding photography



I will only respond to selected applicants, but I truly appreciate your time and interest.



Looking forward to connecting,



Maxime

title: Customer Experience Associate (Part-Time) – Female-Fashion Focused
description:
Role: Part-Time Customer Experience Associate

Location: Remote

Hours: 15–20 hrs/week (Requires flexibility for weekends and USA hours)

Start Date: ASAP



Calily Bridal is a Sydney-based brand offering accessible-luxury wedding veils and bridal accessories. Our team is growing and we’re looking for another warm, thoughtful, and detail-oriented Customer Experience Associate to join us part-time.



About the Role

As the voice of Calily, you’ll be responding to brides through Shopify inbox,
, and social platforms. You will help them choose their dream veil, update them on their orders, and make sure every single message feels personal, supportive, and genuine.



This is not your typical customer service gig - we’re looking for someone who loves love stories, understands the high emotions behind a wedding purchase, and can bring a deeply human touch to every message (all while being highly efficient and detail-oriented!).



What You’ll Do...



Communicate with Care: Provide warm, timely, and thoughtful responses to customer enquiries via Shopify Inbox,
, and socials.



Track Every Detail: Maintain impeccable accuracy when updating customer records, processing orders, and noting specific bridal requests so nothing falls through the cracks (familiarity with 
 for data management is a plus)



Offer Styling Advice: Guide brides on veil styles, timelines, and compatibility with their dresses.



Manage Orders: Handle order updates, returns, and custom requests with clarity and calm.



Be the Brand: Maintain our warm and professional brand tone (following Calily’s tone of voice guidelines).



Problem Solve: Escalate product or shipping issues when needed, while taking full ownership of the customer relationship.



Assist with Content: Occasionally support the team by writing FAQ responses or updating product content.



You’ll Be a Great Fit If You…



Are heavily detail-oriented: You triple-check shipping dates, catch tiny discrepancies in orders, and naturally remember the small personal details brides share with you.



Are a master communicator: You can write beautifully, ensuring every message is clear, perfectly professional, and easy to understand.



Lead with deep empathy: You genuinely care about the brides you help, staying calm under pressure and handling last-minute bride panics with absolute grace.



Have experience in bridal, weddings, or women’s fashion (bonus points if you’ve worked with brides directly).



Are fluent in Shopify and have used customer service tools like Manychat, Gorgias, Zendesk, or similar platforms.



Understand how to turn challenging conversations into loyal, long-term customer relationships.



Work independently, but know exactly when to ask questions or flag issues.



(Bonus!) Have personal experience with veils, bridal accessories, or styling advice.



How to Apply

Please include the following in your application:



1. A short description sharing why you’re excited about this role.



2. An example of how you handled a challenging customer situation.



And at the very top of your application, please write: “I like cats” – this is our way of knowing you read the full job post ??????



We can’t wait to meet someone who shares our passion for heartfelt customer experiences and helping brides feel calm and confident before their big day!

title: Pre-Press / Artwork Technician (Vehicle Graphics)
description:
Role Overview



We are looking for a skilled Pre-Press / Artwork Technician to prepare vehicle graphics and large format designs for print. This role focuses on converting approved designs into accurate, print-ready files, ensuring all artwork meets production standards.



The ideal candidate will have strong experience in Adobe Illustrator, with a solid understanding of pre-press processes, cut paths, scaling, and large format printing.



Key Responsibilities

1. Artwork Preparation

Convert approved designs into print-ready files

Accurately place designs onto vehicle outlines/templates

Ensure correct sizing, scaling, and positioning

2. Cut Paths & Print Setup

Create clean and accurate cut lines (cut paths)

Add bleed, margins, and safe zones where required

Prepare files for large format printing (vehicle wraps, signage, etc.)

3. File Management

Organise and export files in correct formats (AI, PDF, EPS, etc.)

Ensure files are structured and labelled correctly for production

Maintain consistency across all artwork files

4. Quality Control

Check artwork for:

Alignment issues

Resolution problems

Missing elements

Incorrect scaling

Ensure all files meet print production standards before release

5. Collaboration

Work closely with designers and operations team

Flag any issues with designs before print

Support the team in improving artwork accuracy and efficiency

Key Requirements

Strong experience with Adobe Illustrator (essential)

Proven experience in pre-press or print production

Understanding of:

Cut paths

Bleed and margins

Large format printing

Vector artwork

High attention to detail

Ability to work quickly and accurately under deadlines

Desirable (Not Essential)

Experience with vehicle graphics or wrap design

Experience with RIP software or print workflows

Knowledge of signage or large format production

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

Accuracy of print-ready files

Number of files prepared per day/week

Reduction in print errors or reworks

Turnaround time for artwork preparation

Role Expectations

Deliver high-quality, production-ready artwork consistently

Work efficiently to meet daily output targets

Take ownership of file accuracy and print readiness

Communicate clearly with the team when issues arise

title: Video Editor & Social Media Manager
description:
Video Editor & Social Media Manager (Freelance – Remote)

About Us



We are a London-based video production agency working across a range of industries including dentistry, jewellery, small business, and education.

Our work includes website videos, social media content, interview-led brand films, and documentary-style storytelling. Much of our content is narrative-led, meaning we regularly work with long-form interviews that must be crafted into clear, engaging stories for web and social media.

We are looking for a freelance Video Editor & Social Media Manager to join our remote post-production team on an ongoing basis.



Working Hours

Ability to overlap with UK working hours when required

After probation period, the majority of work may be completed during Philippine working hours

This role requires someone who can problem-solve independently while the UK team is offline



Role Overview

This is a dual-role position involving both video editing and social media management.

Alongside editing content created by our production team, you will also be responsible for helping manage and grow social media accounts for our clients using the content we produce.

Content will be filmed and captured by our UK production team. You will be responsible for editing this content and managing its delivery and performance across social platforms.



Video Editor Responsibilities

Role responsibilities include, but are not limited to. Applicants must be able to:

Edit Instagram Reels and TikTok-style social media content

Edit interview-led brand and website videos

Review long-form interviews (e.g. 20 minutes) and craft them into short narrative-led edits (e.g. 2–3 minutes)

Select key soundbites to build a clear story

Multi-cam editing

Sync externally recorded audio with camera footage

Audio levelling and mixing (dialogue, music, sound effects)

Music selection

Basic colour correction

Add subtitles and on-screen text where required

Version edits correctly (e.g. v1a, v1b etc)

Upload and download large footage files overnight when needed

Collaborate with our existing Philippines-based editor across multiple projects

Overlap working hours with other editors when required



Social Media Manager Responsibilities

As Social Media Manager, you will be expected to:

Strategise ways to grow client accounts

Focus on attracting the right followers (not just increasing follower count)

Improve engagement across platforms

Write captions for posts

Manage scheduled posting

Create and post Stories where appropriate

Engage with relevant accounts, brands, and communities

Support the overall growth of client social media presence using video-led content

Required Software & Workflow



Applicants must:

Be proficient in DaVinci Resolve (or willing to transition fully)

Have reliable, high-speed internet capable of:

Downloading large footage files

Uploading exports

Working within DaVinci Cloud projects

Be comfortable working with:

OneDrive

pCloud



Microsoft Teams / Tasks

Requirements



We are looking for someone who:

Has strong attention to detail

Can follow a creative brief without step-by-step direction

Understands pacing for social media platforms

Can make interview-led content feel natural and intentional

Has an eye for detail and storytelling

Is comfortable working remotely to clear deadlines

Can identify and solve problems independently when required

Availability

Freelance role

Initially contracted for approximately 2–3 days per week

Ongoing work available for the right candidate

Application Requirements



Please include:

Portfolio ( examples of relevant work)

Examples of social media accounts you have helped manage or grow

Short introduction

General weekly availability

Details of your internet speed

Any professional references

description:

Writing high quality ad copy that reflects each client's brand voice

Weekly team calls via video

Our own internal systems for reporting and team coordination

Standard Google stack (Ads, Analytics, Tag Manager, Looker Studio, Merchant Center)

Detail oriented, you double check your work

Long term mindset, you want to grow with one agency rather than juggle ten short gigs



How we work together

mbers through Slack and ClickUp. We give you the autonomy to set your own hours and days, in exchange for clear ownership and proactive communication.

title: Sr. Bookkeeper
description:
Job Title: Sr. Bookkeeper

Location: Remote (Work from Home)

Job Type: Full-Time

Timezone: PH (Manila)



Job Summary

We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Senior Bookkeeper to join our team. This role is ideal for a seasoned bookkeeping professional who thrives in a dynamic environment, takes ownership of complex financial records, and brings both technical depth and leadership to every engagement. The Senior Bookkeeper will serve as the subject-matter expert for QuickBooks Online (QBO) and Desktop (QBD), lead clean-up and catch-up projects, oversee systems integrations, and mentor junior staff — all while ensuring our clients receive audit-ready financials and strategic financial visibility.





Key Responsibilities & Expertise

QuickBooks Expertise (QBO & QBD)

Lead migration from QuickBooks Desktop (various versions) to QuickBooks Online

Optimize chart of accounts, classes, and system structure for scalable reporting

Advanced user of QBO Online features, including bank feeds, automation, and reporting tools

Can do Project management in QBO

Nice to have: QBO Certified (ProAdvisor)



Clean-Up & Catch-Up Specialist

Perform full-cycle clean-up of messy books (historical corrections, reclassification, reconciliation)

Resolve discrepancies in bank, credit card, AR/AP, and undeposited funds

Deliver audit-ready financials



System Integrations

Implement and manage payroll integrations (ADP, Gusto, Payroll Relief)

Integrate sales platforms (Shopify, Stripe, Square, PayPal) into QBO

Troubleshoot sync issues and recommend manual workflows when integrations create errors



Financial Reporting & Analysis

Prepare and review monthly financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow)

Ensure accurate classification of income, COGS, and expenses

Support multi-entity and class-based reporting



Process Improvement & Controls

Design and document SOPs for bookkeeping and month-end close

Improve workflows for efficiency, accuracy, and scalability

Maintain strong internal controls and reconciliation processes



Leadership & Training

Train and mentor junior bookkeepers on QBO best practices

Review team output to ensure accuracy and consistency

Standardize workflows across clients using proven QBO methodologies



Client & Team Support

Communicate directly with clients to resolve open items and clarify transactions

Provide strategic recommendations to improve financial visibility



What We Offer

100% remote work environment

US Holiday off

Collaborative and supportive remote team culture

title: Medical Illustration & Design Assistant (AI + Figma/Illustrator)
description:
We are looking for a detail-oriented Design Assistant to support the creation of high-quality educational visuals for a modern medical education platform.



The role involves using AI image generation tools to create scientific and medical illustrations, refining and editing these assets in Adobe Illustrator, and placing them into pre-designed visual templates in Figma. You will also be responsible for adjusting typography, labels, layouts, colours, and other design components to ensure consistency with our brand aesthetic.



This is not purely a graphic design role — we are looking for someone who has an eye for clean, sophisticated educational visuals and can work carefully with medical/scientific content.



Responsibilities:



* Generate medical and scientific illustrations using AI image generation tools

* Refine and edit illustrations in Adobe Illustrator

* Organise and place visuals into existing Figma templates

* Adjust labels, typography, spacing, colours, and layouts for consistency

* Ensure illustrations are clear, visually polished, and educationally useful

* Follow brand guidelines and maintain a premium visual standard

* Collaborate with the team to improve workflows and output quality



Requirements:



* Strong proficiency in Adobe Illustrator

* Good working knowledge of Figma

* Experience with AI image generation tools

* Strong attention to detail and layout consistency

* Good understanding of typography and visual hierarchy

* Ability to follow structured templates and systems

* Good communication and reliability



Bonus Skills (Preferred but not Required):



* Experience creating medical, anatomical, or scientific illustrations

* Background in biomedical science, medicine, or healthcare design

* Experience preparing educational or textbook-style graphics

* Familiarity with modern AI creative workflows

We are building a modern medical education platform focused on creating a cleaner, more refined learning experience for medical students and junior doctors. Visual quality and clarity are extremely important to us, and we are building a long-term team around these standards.

title: Ad Creative Specialist
description:
About us



We are a fast-growing direct-response e-commerce brand, selling health, wellness, beauty, and supplement products to Scandinavian-speaking audiences. We operate on a performance-first model — we test fast, scale what works, and cut what doesn’t. Our traffic runs primarily through Meta 
/Instagram) and every creative we put in front of an audience is built to convert.



We’re a lean, remote team with big ambitions. If you want to work in one of the fastest-moving corners of digital marketing and see the direct impact of your work in real sales numbers, this is the right place.



The Role



We are hiring a full-time Ad Creative Specialist to be the engine behind our static ad production. Your primary job is to take competitor ad swipes collected by our research team and recreate them as polished, conversion-optimised static creatives adapted for our products and Scandinavian-speaking audience.



This is not a brand design role. It is a direct-response creative role. Every asset you produce is tested against a real audience with real ad spend behind it. Speed, consistency, and attention to detail matter more than artistic flair.



Output expectation

Up to 100 approved static creatives per month across all active products. As we scale, output expectations scale with them — and so does your compensation.



Responsibilities



Receive weekly swipe batches from the Ad Swipe Researcher and recreate them as static ad creatives adapted for our products

Produce up to 8 approved static creatives per active product per week

Adapt English-language competitor ads for Scandinavian -speaking audiences — translating copy, localising tone, and adjusting visuals where needed

Ensure every creative matches the product it represents: correct claims, correct imagery, correct branding

Apply direct-response principles to every asset: clear hook, clear benefit, clear CTA

Review creatives against Meta advertising policy before submission — flag any compliance concerns proactively

Deliver completed creatives in the agreed format and folder structure by the agreed weekly deadline

Stay current on static ad trends and formats performing well on Meta



What This Role Is Not



To be clear about scope:



No video editing — static image creatives only

No competitor research — swipes are provided to you by the research team

No copywriting from scratch — copy direction comes from the swipe and product brief

No brand strategy — you execute briefs, you don’t set direction



Qualifications & Skills



Proven experience creating static ad creatives for paid social, ideally for e-commerce or dropshipping brands

Experience with AI tools is a MUST — you are expected to use AI to speed up production, not avoid it

Proficiency in Canva and/or Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator) — you should be fast in at least one of these

Strong understanding of direct-response advertising: you know the difference between a brand ad and a conversion ad

Familiarity with Meta advertising formats, specs, and policy requirements

Detail-oriented — wrong product name, wrong price, wrong claim = rejected creative

Able to work independently, manage your own output queue, and hit weekly deadlines without being chased

Comfortable working fast: this is a volume role with quality standards, not a slow-craft role

Previous experience working for dropshipping or direct-response e-commerce companies is a strong plus







Who You Are



Beyond the skills, we hire for character. If the following describes you, keep reading:



You do what you say. You finish what you start. Deadlines are not suggestions.

You have an obsessive attention to detail. Nothing gets past you — and we mean nothing. (Seriously: include “the devil is in the details” as your answer to the first application question. If you skip this, your application will not be reviewed.)

You are results-driven. You want to see your creatives perform, not just look good.

You take ownership. If something is wrong, you flag it. You don’t wait to be told.



Compensation



Component



Trainee period (Month 1)

$200 flat / month

One-month trial — evaluated at end of month

Full-time (from Month 2)

$300 flat / month

Up to 100 approved creatives/month

Overage

$1.50 / creative

Per approved creative above 100 in a calendar month

Payment schedule

Every 2 weeks

Invoiced bi weekly, decided upon hiring





Active products are defined as any product with at least one live Meta campaign in that calendar month. Scale increases apply automatically — no renegotiation needed.



Transparency on earnings

At current scale (3 active products, ~24 creatives/week), your base is $200/month. As we grow to 10+ active products running 8 creatives/week each, your monthly earnings scale accordingly. This is a long-term role built to grow with the business.



Trial Period



We always start with a one-month paid trial at $200/month. At the end of month one, we evaluate together: output quality, speed, reliability, and communication. If both parties want to continue, we move to the full arrangement outlined above.



The trial is a two-way street — it protects you as much as it protects us.

If it is clear we don’t match after the first payment cycle the collaboration will be discontinued.



How to Apply



Send your application with the following:



Answer to the first question (you know what it is)

2–3 examples of static ad creatives you have made for e-commerce or paid social campaigns

Brief description of your experience with dropshipping or direct-response brands

Tools you use (Canva, Photoshop, AI tools, etc.)

Your availability and expected start date



Applications without portfolio examples or the attention test answer will not be reviewed.

title: Video Editor
description:
We are looking for a skilled video editor to own the post-production workflow for both long-form and short-form content. Someone who brings technical precision and aesthetic sensibility to content about healing, consciousness, and medicine.



This is a remote, part-time role with consistent weekly work. Volume increases as the channel grows.



WHAT YOU'LL EDIT



- YouTube long-form (10-20 min talking-head + B-roll): color grade, audio cleanup, typography/titles, subtitles, pacing

- Instagram Reels + TikTok (30-90 sec): masking, motion typography, text overlays, B-roll integration, hook-optimized cuts

- YouTube Shorts: vertical reformats from long-form, punchy cuts, subtitle styling



MUST-HAVE SKILLS



- Advanced masking — background replacement, subject isolation, motion tracking

- Typography + kinetic text — on-brand title cards, pull quotes, lower thirds

- Subtitle/caption generation and styling (burned-in, not just SRT)

- Color correction and grading — fixing mixed lighting, skin tone accuracy, cinematic look

- Audio cleanup — noise reduction, leveling, music ducking

- Brand consistency — maintaining the same look/feel across all platforms



Software: Proficiency in Filmora required (our primary editing tool). Familiarity with Riverside.fm strongly preferred — understanding how to work with multi-track Riverside exports is a big plus. Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve experience welcome.



NICE TO HAVE



- Experience with wellness, health, or educational content

- Motion graphics (After Effects or Filmora effects)

- Familiarity with teleprompter-style talking-head footage

- Vertical-first content experience (Reels/TikTok)



THE AESTHETIC



Clean. Warm. Credible. Not flashy. Think: calm authority, subtle motion, typography that adds meaning rather than noise. References: Andrew Huberman Lab, Mark Hyman, Sam Harris. Healing content that looks like it was made with intention.

title: Experienced Credit Repair Virtual Assistant (Part-Time | Immediate Start)
description:
Growing U.S.-based credit consulting company seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Credit Repair Virtual Assistant for immediate hire.



This is not an entry-level position. I am looking for someone who understands the credit repair process, can work independently, and can assist in managing disputes, compliance-related tasks, and client file processing with accuracy and urgency.



Responsibilities Include:



* Prepare and organize dispute letters for all three bureaus

* Experience using DisputeFox (or similar CRM/software)

* Assist with factual-based disputes, identity theft disputes, and supporting documentation

* Submit and track complaints through:

    * Better Business Bureau (BBB)

    * Federal Trade Commission (FTC)

    * Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) preferred

* Review credit reports and identify negative items for dispute strategy

* Update client files and maintain organized records

* Help monitor dispute responses and reinvestigation results

* Support client communication and case follow-up

* Assist with administrative support related to credit restoration services



Hours per week and salary are negotiable



Qualifications:



* Prior credit repair industry experience REQUIRED

* Familiarity with Metro 2, FCRA, FDCPA, and dispute processes preferred

* Experience using DisputeFox strongly preferred

* Strong English written communication

* Detail-oriented and organized

* Able to meet deadlines and follow SOPs

* Self-starter with integrity and accountability

* Can begin immediately



Ideal Candidate:

I am looking for someone proactive, reliable, and experienced—someone who understands urgency and takes ownership of their work. This role may grow into a long-term position for the right person.



To Apply, Please Include:



1. Your credit repair experience

2. Software/tools you’ve used (DisputeFox, Client Dispute Manager, etc.)

3. Experience with BBB, FTC, or CFPB complaints

4. Examples of dispute strategies you have used

5. Your availability to start immediately



Important:

Please do not apply if you do not have direct credit repair experience.

title: Business Relationship Manager
description:
We are looking for a dynamic Business relationship manager who is passionate about building relationships, identifying opportunities, and helping the company grow.



In this role, you will be a key contributor to expanding our client base and strengthening our market presence across multiple business divisions. You will engage with prospects, understand their needs, and present solutions that add value. You will work closely with management and internal teams to ensure seamless execution—from initial outreach to closing deals and maintaining strong client relationships.



If you are proactive, confident, and enjoy a fast-paced environment where your performance directly drives business success, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities



Sales and Business Development

· Identify and develop new business opportunities through networking, prospecting, referrals, partnerships, and high-end professional channels.



· Promote and sell the company’s full suite of corporate services, including:

company incorporation, corporate secretarial services, compliance, accounting, tax, employment passes, substance packages, and banking and business advisory solutions.



· Conduct sales presentations, client meetings, and follow-ups to understand business needs and propose suitable service packages.



· Proactively acquire premium clientele, including HNW individuals, global entrepreneurs, investors, and businesses seeking international structuring or family office support.



· Build a strong pipeline of high-quality leads and consistently achieve monthly and quarterly sales targets.



· Develop and maintain a strong sales pipeline to achieve monthly and quarterly sales targets.



· Increase client lifetime value and ensure clients benefit from RSBU’s one-stop solution model.



Lead Qualification & Sales Cycle Management



· Qualify leads to ensure they meet RSBU’s ideal client profile and service standards.



· Manage the full sales cycle from first contact to contract signing and handover to operational teams.



· Conduct needs analysis and propose tailored solutions for incorporation, visas, substance, and compliance.



· Ensure timely follow-ups to maximise conversion rates.



Client Relationship Management

· Build and maintain long-term relationships with clients, partners, intermediaries, and referral networks.



· Act as the primary contact for client inquiries and assist with understanding regulatory requirements.



· Provide a premium customer experience aligned with RSBU’s brand standards.



· Support clients through onboarding and coordinate with internal teams to ensure seamless service delivery.



Market Research & Strategy

· Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and industry developments to identify growth opportunities.



· Contribute to marketing initiatives, sales campaigns, and strategic planning for business expansion.



· Provide feedback to management on customer needs, service improvement opportunities, and product development.



Documentation & Compliance Coordination

· Prepare proposals, quotations, service agreements, and sales documentation.



· Ensure accurate input of client information and sales activities into CRM systems.



· Generate weekly/monthly reports on pipeline, performance, and KPIs.



· Adhere to PDPA requirements and internal compliance standards.



· Support the compliance process by gathering required documents for KYC/AML checks.



Key Requirements

Professional Qualification



· Diploma or Degree in Business, Law, Finance, Marketing, or related fields preferred.



· Preferably 1–3 years of experience in business development, sales, corporate services, legal, banking, finance or related professional services.



Skills and Competencies



· Familiarity with corporate governance, company incorporation processes, compliance, or relevant corporate frameworks is an advantage.



· Strong communication, negotiation, presentation, and interpersonal skills.



· Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritise effectively, and work independently.



· Self-driven, proactive, and professional, with a strong service-oriented mindset and commercial awareness.



· Confidence interacting with CEOs, founders, investors, and high-net-worth clients.



Salary & Benefits

· Salary commensurate with experience and skills



· Supportive and professional working environment



· Opportunities for learning, development, and potential future career progression



· Exposure to a multi-business environment (corporate services, travel, events, etc.)



Interested candidates are invited to submit their comprehensive resume, including:



· Experience



· Availability



· Current salary

title: Executive Assistant to CEO
description:
Position Overview:



As the Executive Assistant to the CEO, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the smooth and efficient functioning of the executive office. Your primary responsibility will be to provide comprehensive administrative support to the CEO, managing their schedule, and coordinating various tasks to enhance productivity. Your ability to maintain confidentiality, exercise sound judgment, and proactively handle administrative duties will contribute to the overall effectiveness of the CEO's office.





Pay Range: $8 per hour with a performance review scheduled after 90 days of employment. We believe in rewarding exceptional talents.





About Power Plus Systems:



Power Plus Systems LLC has been a dominant force in the residential electrical contracting industry for two decades, and we continue to lead the way. Our vision is to successfully wire 28,000 homes to completion by the year 2029, ensuring safe and efficient electrical solutions, thereby making a positive impact on the lives of our customers and communities. At Power Plus Systems, LLC, a position with us isn't just a job; it's an opportunity to grow and develop within a company that genuinely appreciates your contributions and dedication to our shared mission.





Objectives:

• Manage and organize the CEO's daily schedule, including meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements.

• Act as a gatekeeper, screening and prioritizing communications to the CEO.

• Coordinate and facilitate communication between the CEO and internal/external stakeholders.

• Anticipate the CEO's needs and proactively handle tasks to ensure optimal time management.

• Coordinate special projects and initiatives as assigned by the CEO.





Competencies:

• Exceptional organizational and multitasking skills, with keen attention to detail.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills.

• Ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise discretion in handling sensitive information.

• Proficient in using Microsoft Office and Google Suites.

• Familiarity with scheduling software; experience with additional productivity tools is an advantage.

• Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with individuals at all levels.

• Proactive problem solver and resourceful thinker.





Equipment Requirements:

• Must own a reliable computer or laptop capable of handling administrative tasks efficiently.

• Must own and actively use an iPhone and/or MacBook or Mac computer for efficient communication and task management.

• High-speed internet connection to support video calls, cloud-based collaboration, and remote work.



Physical Requirements:

• Extended periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.



Benefits:

• Growth opportunities in a well-established company.

• Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.

• Opportunity for advancement within the company.

• A supportive and collaborative work environment.

• Company-observed paid United States holidays (full time).

• Paid vacation.





How to Apply:



If you are ready to join Power Plus Systems, LLC, a well-established residential electrical contractor that values its tea
mbers and offers excellent benefits, please submit your application through the link below.




/internationalcareers



Power Plus Systems, LLC is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds to apply.



Join us at Power Plus Systems, LLC, and be part of our continued success as we embark on the next 20 years of excellence in residential electrical contracting!

title: Financial Reporting Analyst
description:
The Financial Reporting Analyst provides high?quality financial analysis, reporting, and assurance material to support executive decision?making, Board reporting, and internal control frameworks. The role focuses on turning complex financial, contractual, and operational data into clear, decision?ready insights.



Key responsibilities include preparing Board?ready financial reports and executive summaries; supporting revenue assurance, contract reconciliation, and budget analysis; producing variance analysis, trend analysis, and forward?looking insights; and developing clear, evidence?based commentary aligned to governance and audit expectations. The analyst also supports internal audit cycles, assurance dashboards, and compliance with New Zealand reporting standards and organisational policies.



The role requires strong analytical capability, excellent written communication, and the ability to work independently across large datasets and fragmented information sources. Outputs must be concise, accurate, and suitable for senior executives, audit committees, and Boards.



Must have a strong experience and ability with Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel. Experience with Xero beneficial but not required.  



Inititally looking for part-time, but role could expand to full-time for the right candidate.

title: Video Editor – Movie Breakdown Videos (Shift Creative Media)
description:
Job Title: Video Editor – Movie Breakdown Videos

Type of Work: Part Time (project-based, no minimum hours guarantee)

Rate: $7.50-10 USD/hour (based on experience and speed)



About Us

Shift Creative Media runs multiple YouTube channels focused on movie breakdowns, analysis, and deep dives. We're growing fast and need a skilled, efficient video editor who can deliver quality work quickly.

This is consistent weekly work (2 videos/week) for someone who values efficiency and wants to grow with us long-term.

We don't want slow editors who need hand-holding. We want professionals who can execute.



What You'll Actually Be Doing

You'll edit 2 movie breakdown videos per week (12-20 minutes final length each).

We provide:

Voiceover audio (already recorded)

Movie clips and relevant footage

Clear written instructions for each video

Keyword list with timestamps for text pop-ups

You do:

Sync voiceover to footage (timing is critical)

Add keyword text pop-ups at specific 
nts

Use provided movie clips as B-roll/filler footage

Ensure smooth pacing and flow

Export final video (1080p minimum)

Track your hours honestly

You are NOT responsible for:

Recording voiceover

Finding your own footage

Writing scripts or creative direction

Thumbnails (separate role)

Feature

$7.50-10/hour

Above market rate for PH video editors

2 videos/week

Predictable workflow, not burnout

No minimum hours

Work efficiently, earn more per hour

Assets provided

No hunting for clips

Long-term

No hunting for new clients



We want an editor who can deliver a polished 20-minute breakdown in 6-8 hours. If you're slow, this isn't for you. If you're efficient, you'll make great money.



Requirements

Ability to sync voiceover to footage precisely

Attention to detail on text placement and timing

Reliable internet (you'll download/upload large files)

Good English reading comprehension

Portfolio required (YouTube links preferred)

Honest time tracking

Nice to have:

Experience with YouTube content

Basic understanding of pacing and retention

Dealbreakers:

Inconsistent communication or disappearing for days

Missing deadlines without notice

Taking 10+ hours to edit a 15-minute video with provided assets

Inflated time reporting



Pay & Hours

Detail

Information

Hourly rate

$7.50-10 USD/hour

Hours per week

Varies based on your speed (typically 12-18 hours for 2 videos)

Videos per week

2 (guaranteed)

Weekly pay (estimated)

90?180(based on 12?18 hours

90?180(based on 12?18 hoursat7.50-10)

Monthly pay (estimated)

$
Payment method

Fiverr (buyer protection) 

Schedule

Flexible, but need daily communication



You are paid for actual time worked, not a guaranteed minimum. Work efficiently and you earn a higher effective hourly rate.



How to Apply (Follow EXACTLY)

Step 1: Subject line must be: SCM Editor - [Your Name]

Step 2: First line of your message: "RK sent me"

Step 3: Answer these questions:

What editing software do you use?

How many hours does it typically take you to edit a 15-minute YouTube video with provided VO and clips? (Be honest)

Shortlisted candidates will receive a 2-minute test clip + VO. Complete the edit within 48 hours. Paid $15 upon submission.

This test task helps us verify your speed estimate. If you claim 6 hours but take 12, we'll know.



What Happens Next

We review all submissions (3-5 days)

Top candidates receive the paid test task

Test task submissions reviewed (speed + quality)

Final candidates: brief text/voice interview

Offer extended to one editor

2-week paid trial (you track hours, I verify reasonableness)

If successful: ongoing weekly commitment (2 videos/week)



Why Work With Shift Creative Media?

Consistent work — 2 videos/week guaranteed

Above-market rates ($7.50-10/hour for skilled editors)

No minimum hours — work efficiently, earn more per hour

Ready to apply? Follow the steps exactly. We look forward to seeing your work.

— RK, Shift Creative Media

title: GTM and AI Engineer
description:
GTM and AI Engineer 



SalesSourcers · Remote · Full-time



We're hiring a rare hybrid: half growth operator, half builder. Someone who can spin up a Clay workflow before lunch and ship a Cursor-built internal tool by dinner. If you treat outbound like an engineering problem and AI like a coworker, keep reading.



What you'll actually do



Build, test, and scale outbound and lead-gen systems that don't suck

Architect lead lists from scratch — sourcing, enriching, scoring, and segmenting in Clay (and whatever else gets the job done)

Identify and operationalize buying signals: job changes, funding rounds, tech-stack shifts, hiring patterns, intent data

Stand up automations across the full GTM stack — CRM, sequencer, enrichment, dialer, reporting

Build internal tools, integrations, and lightweight apps with Cursor + Claude — think AI-powered list scoring, deal-hygiene bots, dedupe pipelines, custom dashboards

Turn messy CSV exports and half-broken Zapier flows into clean, observable systems

Partner with sales leadership to translate "we need more meetings with X" into shippable infrastructure

Keep the team honest with data — no vibes-based pipeline forecasts



What you bring



2+ years in a GTM, RevOps, growth engineering, or sales engineering role at a B2B company

Clay obsession — not "I've used Clay," but "I've built tables that do unhinged things"

Hands-on with the modern outbound stack: Apollo, Smartlead / Instantly, HubSpot or Salesforce, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Hunter, and friends

Comfortable in code — JavaScript or Python, plus working knowledge of APIs, webhooks, and basic data modeling

You've shipped real things using Cursor and Claude (or similar AI-native dev tools) — not just toy projects

You've built actual integrations — connected two systems that didn't talk to each other and made them behave

Strong instinct for buying signals and how to weaponize them at scale

You think in funnels, edge cases, and unit economics



Bonus points



You've worked at a sales agency, outbound-heavy SaaS, or a PLG-to-sales-led company

You can prompt-engineer your way out of most problems

You've built custom GPTs, MCP servers, or AI agents that do real work



Why this seat is worth taking



Real ownership — this isn't a "one of fifteen RevOps analysts" gig

Direct line to the founder; your work will shape how the company operates

Budget for tools, AI subscriptions, and the gear you need to ship

Remote-first, async-friendly, output-driven

A company that's actually growing — not "growing"



How to apply

Send all of the below to 



A 1-minute video explaining why you're a fit — start it by saying "bananas." No bananas, no review.

A short note on the most interesting GTM system you've built — Clay table, automation, internal tool, anything

A GitHub link, Loom, or anything that proves you ship

Your favorite buying signal and how you'd act on it



No cover letters. No corporate-speak. Show us what you've built.

This role works closely with Cloud, Sales, and Vendor teams  into high impact customer communications that drive awareness, engagement, and pipeline. 



 



Key Responsibilities 



Campaign & EDM Execution 

-Plan, build, and deploy EDM campaigns for new vendor partnerships and cloud solutions 

-Translate internal campaign requests into clear, compelling customer facing messaging 

-Manage end to end EDM delivery including copy, layout, approvals, scheduling, and reporting 



Vendor & Stakeholder Collaboration 

-Partner with Cloud Business Managers, Sales, and Vendors to align messaging and timing 

-Ensure vendor brand guidelines and co branding requirements are followed 

-Coordinate approvals with internal stakeholders and external vendors 



Content Development 

-Draft EDM copy including subject lines, headlines, CTAs, and supporting content 

-Adapt messaging for different audiences (customers, partners, internal sales) 

-Support creation of landing pages, campaign briefs, and sales enablement assets 



Campaign Performance & Optimisation 

-Track and report on EDM performance (open rates, CTR, conversions) 

-Optimise subject lines, content, and CTAs based on performance insights 

-Maintain campaign documentation and best practice templates 



Key Deliverables 



-Vendor partnership announcement EDMs 

-Campaign briefs and messaging frameworks 

-Performance reports and insights 

-Co branded marketing assets 



 

Skills & Experience 



Essential 



-3+ years’ experience in B2B marketing, preferably in IT, cloud, or technology 

-Hands on experience with EDM/email marketing platforms (e.g. Dynamics, HubSpot, Marketo, Mailchimp) 

-Strong copywriting and messaging skills 

-Proven ability to manage multiple campaigns and deadlines 

-Stakeholder management experience across sales, marketing, and vendors 





Desirable 

-Experience working with cloud, SaaS, or vendor led marketing programs 

-Understanding of channel or partner marketing models 

-Basic knowledge of CRM and marketing automation 



 

Key Competencies 



-Strong communication and attention to detail 

-Commercial mindset with focus on pipeline impact 

-Ability to simplify technical messaging for business audiences 

-Highly organised and deadline driven 



 

Success Measures 



-On time delivery of campaign requests 

-Engagement and performance of EDM campaigns 

-Sales and vendor satisfaction with campaign execution 

-Contribution to pipeline and vendor program success

title: SEO Strategist
description:
SEO Strategist



Hey! We’re Dilate, a result driven digital marketing agency with an impressive, diverse client list and a world-class, global team.

It’s our mission to do great marketing, for great businesses, ethically and in a way that benefits us all. We want our clients to win, their customers to win, and our employees to win.



We’ve got pretty ambitious growth plans and we’re looking for an SEO Strategist to join our team.



We are looking for someone with an in-depth knowledge of SEO, a strong understanding of search intent and algorithms, a keen eye for detail, and a focus on driving measurable growth through organic search. Ideally, you will have experience working agency-side, managing SEO strategies for multiple clients across different industries.



This opportunity is remote working from home, full-time Monday to Friday 9–5 pm AWST (the successful candidate must be available during these hours) as we are an agency located in Perth, Western Australia.



So, the job…

* Develop and execute SEO strategies that align with client goals and deliver measurable organic growth.

* Conduct keyword research, competitor analysis, and technical SEO audits to identify opportunities.

* Optimise on-page SEO elements including content, metadata, internal linking, and site structure.

* Analyse SEO performance using tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and SEO platforms.

* Monitor rankings, traffic, and conversions, and adjust strategies to improve performance and ROI.

* Stay up to date with SEO trends, algorithm updates, and best practices.

* Provide clear, data-driven insights and strategic recommendations to clients and internal teams.

* Collaborate with content, development, and paid media teams to support integrated marketing strategies.





Your skills:

* Excellent written and spoken English.

* Strong understanding of technical SEO, on-page SEO, and off-page SEO fundamentals.

* Experience using SEO tools such as Google Search Console, Google Analytics, Ahrefs, SEMrush, or similar.

* Proven track record of improving organic traffic, rankings, and conversions.

* Solid understanding of search intent and content optimisation.

* Good instincts for conversion rate optimisation and maximising website performance.

* Ability to translate data into actionable SEO strategies and clear recommendations.

* Strategic thinker with the ability to plan long-term growth while executing tactically.

* Ability to work independently and as part of a team, with a proactive, solutions-focused mindset.

* Hungry for knowledge, learning, and personal development.

* At least 3mbps of stable internet connection (with a backup internet).

* MUST have a mid-level performance desktop or laptop (good for multitasking).

* MUST have a working mic & webcam.



This is a great opportunity to be a part of a fast growing agency.

If you feel that this is the job for you, we would love to hear from you! 

Please include your resume and cover letter.



Thanks for your time,

The Dilate Digital Team

title: Accounting Manager
description:
Job Summary



The Accounting Manager is responsible for overseeing the daily operations of the accounting department, ensuring accurate financial reporting, compliance with company policies, and adherence to applicable accounting standards and government regulations. The role includes managing financial records, supervising accounting staff, and supporting management in financial planning and decision-making.



Key Duties and Responsibilities



Manage and supervise the day-to-day operations of the accounting department.

Prepare and review monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements and reports.

Ensure timely and accurate recording of all financial transactions.

Monitor accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll, and general ledger activities.

Oversee bank reconciliation and cash flow management

Ensure compliance with tax requirements, statutory reporting, and government remittances.

Prepare documents and schedules for internal and external audits.

Develop and implement accounting policies, procedures, and internal controls.

Analyze financial data and provide recommendations to management.

Assist in budget preparation, forecasting, and financial planning.

Supervise, train, and evaluate accounting staff performance and ensure confidentiality and security of financial records.



Skills and Attributes



Strong leadership and team management skills

Analytical and critical thinking ability

Good communication skills

Ability to meet deadlines and work under pressure

High level of integrity and confidentiality



Qualifications



Graduate of a Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

At least 4-6 years of experience in accounting, with 2 years in a supervisory or managerial role

Strong knowledge of accounting principles, taxation, and financial reporting

Strong analytical, leadership, and problem-solving skills

Excellent communication and organizational skills



Interested Applicants may send their Resume/CV on our
:

title: **B2B Appointment Setter (Cold Calling) – Base + Commission | US Clients | Long-Term Role

We are a fast-growing US-based company looking for highly motivated B2B Appointment Setters to join our team.



Your role is simple:

1.  Make outbound calls to business owners and decision-makers

2.  Get them interested

3.  Book appointments for our sales team



We provide:



Proven scripts

Targeted lead lists

Clear daily targets

Long-term opportunity for growth



If you are confident on the phone and love closing appointments, this is for you.



What You’ll Be Calling About:



You will represent two strong business offers:



1. Achieve Sales Alliance (ASA)

Helping companies scale their sales teams with trained commission-based sales professionals



2. Trio Insurance (USA)

Helping business owners save money on Workers’ Compensation Insurance



*** You are NOT selling — you are booking qualified appointments.



Responsibilities:

Make 80–150 outbound calls per day

Speak with business owners, managers, or decision-makers

Handle gatekeepers confidently

Use our script but sound natural and conversational

Book appointments directly into our calendar

Update call notes in CRM (GoHighLevel or similar)

Compensation:

+PLUS Commission



*** Top performers can earn $800–$1,500+/month



Requirements:

Excellent English speaking skills (clear, confident, neutral accent)

Experience in cold calling / appointment setting / telemarketing

Comfortable handling rejection and objections

Strong internet connection + quiet workspace

USB headset and computer/laptop

Ability to work US business hours 

(Philippine time will be around 10Pm onwards)



Big PLUS if you have:

B2B calling experience

Insurance or staffing campaign experience

Experience using dialers / CRM systems

Track record of hitting call or booking targets

What We’re Looking For:



We are NOT looking for average callers.



We want someone who:



Is competitive and driven by results

Can think on their feet

Doesn’t give up after rejection

Wants to grow into a team leader or closer role

How to Apply (IMPORTANT):



To be considered, follow ALL steps:



Send a short voice recording (30–60 seconds) introducing yourself

Share your past experience with cold calling or appointment setting



Applications without voice recordings will NOT be considered.



Final Note:



This is a long-term opportunity with room for growth, bonuses, and leadership roles.



If you are serious about earning and growing, apply now.

title: Head of Talent Acquisition / Head of Hiring

You'll hire across 30 to 50 roles per year: editors, writers, thumbnail designers, producers, operators, and senior strategic hires. Volume and senior roles run differently. Part of the job is building two distinct playbooks and knowing when to use which.





What this role is not:

- A coordination role buried under process

- A role where you wait for approval to make obvious improvements

- A role for someone who needs a large team handed to them on day one

- 5+ years in recruiting or talent acquisition, with at least 2 years owning or building a hiring function

- Real experience hiring at pace across 20+ concurrent roles

- Sourcing fluency on LinkedIn, Upwork,
/X, 
, Reddit, OnlineJobs.ph, and similar platforms

- Demonstrated use of AI tools in recruiting workflows (not theoretical, actually deployed)

- Experience managing a direct report and holding them to a standard

Nice to have: 

Background in YouTube, creator economy, or digital media. 

Experience hiring creative roles. 

Global hiring across timezones.

Full-time contractor, fully remote.

title: Klaviyo Email Marketing Expert
description:
We're not looking for someone who's still learning.

We're not looking for someone who needs checking in on.

We're looking for a Klaviyo
 designer who is genuinely excellent at what they do and can prove it.

If that's you, keep reading.



About Us

I'm Jess, founder of In Flow Marketing, a done-for-you Klaviyo
 marketing agency based on the Gold Coast, Australia.

We work with product-based eCommerce brands to build
 into their most reliable sales channel. Our team is international, fully remote, and high-performing. We move fast, hold high standards, and care deeply about the work we put out.

We don't do average here.



About the Role

This is a contractor position designing Klaviyo
s across multiple eCommerce brands and industries.



You'll work directly alongside me (strategist and copywriter) to bring campaigns and flows to life in Klaviyo, building
s that are brand-consistent, mobile-optimised, deliverability-friendly, and genuinely beautiful.



You'll use Canva for graphic design and image assets, but
s must be assembled directly inside Klaviyo using a mix of native text blocks and image sections. Single full-image imports are not how we work, and you'll understand exactly why.



What You'll Be Doing

-Designing
s directly in Klaviyo for multiple eCommerce clients

-Using Canva for graphic design and image asset creation

-Following brand guidelines with precision, no interpretation required, just execution

-Balancing Klaviyo text blocks and image sections (you know why this matters for deliverability)

-Ensuring mobile optimisation across every design

-Maintaining strong brand consistency across different clients and industries

-Communicating clearly via Slack and ClickUp

-Delivering work on time, without reminders



What We're Looking For

-Proven Klaviyo experience, not Mailchimp, not "similar tools". Klaviyo.

-A portfolio that demonstrates exceptional
 design (not just pretty graphics)

-Solid understanding of
 deliverability and why text-to-image ratio matters

-Obsessive attention to detail and brand consistency

-Ability to take clear feedback and implement it correctly, first time

-Reliable, independent, and deadline-driven

-Available during AEST business hours

-Strong written English



This Is NOT the Role for You If:

-You need step-by-step direction or regular check-ins to complete your work

-You don't understand what deliverability means or why it affects design decisions

-Your portfolio doesn't include real Klaviyo
 examples

-You miss deadlines or go quiet when things get complicated

-You take feedback personally instead of implementing it



We're a professional team doing professional work. If you're still building your skills or looking for a supportive learning environment, this isn't that. We wish you well, but this role isn't a fit.



How to Apply

Complete the application form here: 

The first thing we look at is your design work. Applications without a strong portfolio will not be shortlisted. No exceptions.



Shortlisted applicants will be invited to complete a short paid trial before any ongoing engagement is confirmed.



Good luck, we'd love to find our next tea
mber in you.

Jess

Founder, In Flow Marketing

title: UGC / Influencer Manager
description:
UGC / Influencer Manager (Creative Scale + Brand Trust Layer)





Core Role:



Build and scale a structured UGC + influencer system to improve brand trust, diversify creative output, and reduce over-reliance on AI-generated ads.



Context / Problem to Solve:

• Currently ~80–90% of creatives are AI-based.

• This creates:

     • Potential sustainability risks (platform rejections, fatigue).

     • Weaker brand trust and recognition.

• UGC pipeline is manual and limited (no systems, tools, or scale).



Goal:



Shift toward a balanced creative ecosystem (~50% UGC / 50% AI) while increasing overall creative volume and speed.



Key Responsibilities:

• Build a scalable UGC pipeline (creators, influencers, affiliates).

• Improve and manage current UGC workflow (UGC coordinator reports into this role).

• Transition sourcing from manual outreach ? tool-driven / systemized approach.

• Develop a reliable creator network for consistent content output.

• Coordinate with CS team to ensure fast script ? creator ? asset turnaround.

• Ensure UGC content is:

     • Aligned with performance angles.

     • Usable for paid ads (not just organic content).

• Increase speed of execution (faster turnaround from concept to asset).

• Work with media buyers + CS to feed high-performing UGC into paid channels.

• Test different creator types, formats, and hooks for scalability.



Qualifications:

• Strong experience in UGC, influencer marketing, or affiliate systems.

• Familiar with creator sourcing tools, CRMs, or automation workflows.

• Understands how to produce UGC that converts (direct response, not just aesthetic).

• Experience managing creator relationships and deliverables at scale.

• Ability to build and optimize systems, not just execute manually.

• Strong sense of content quality, hooks, and messaging.

• Comfortable working cross-functionally with CS and media buying teams.

• Bonus: Experience in affiliate programs or PR partnerships.



Objective:



Build a UGC engine that:

• Balances AI-heavy creative output.

• Improves brand trust and authenticity.

• Increases creative volume and speed.

• Directly contributes to better ad performance and scalable spend.



Please submit your application at zurl.to/7umd?source=CareerSite.

Salary



Base salary + $ per booked appointment + 2-4% commission per close.



Realistic earnings: $800-$2,500/month part-time, $2,500-$6,000/month full-time



We will adjust compensation based on performance to make sure you are well compensated based on results.





About Us



Heavy Duty Leads helps US contractor businesses (concrete, roofing, plumbing, HVAC, septic) get more customers through online marketing. Our clients pay $5,750-$18,750 for our services.



We need appointment setters to call US business owners and book discovery calls.





The Role



• Make 50-100 cold calls per day to US contractor business owners

• Book 10-20 qualified discovery calls per week

• Follow up with warm leads via
 and phone

• Log all activity in our CRM





Work Hours



US business hours required. You pick your schedule, but calls must happen when US business owners are available:

• 8am - 6pm Eastern Time (8pm - 6am Manila time)

• Minimum 4-hour shifts

• 20-40 hours per week





Requirements - Must Have



• Sales or cold calling experience (any industry)

• Confident speaking voice in English (the less accent the better)

• Reliable internet (25+ Mbps) and quiet space for calls

• Available during US business hours (though I don’t care what hours you work)

• Can handle rejection and keep dialing





Requirements - Nice to Have



• Experience calling US businesses

• B2B sales background

• Familiar with CRM tools





Compensation Details



Base Pay: Negotiable based on experience.



Per Appointment (shows only).



Note: We're building this system and will adjust compensation to make sure top performers are well paid. If you perform, you will earn.





How to Apply



Apply here: 
/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScHsxVmuD_JKZ00xKh1XtGEMSZsb6J9JEiidzWCrwVZJNMqdQ/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=
Your application must include:



1. Loom video (3-5 min, face on camera) with:

• Introduction about yourself and your cold calling experience

• Mock cold call to a roofing company owner named Mike



2. Your OnlineJobs.ph profile link



3. Expected hourly rate



4. Reasonably availability in US Eastern Time



We review applications within 48 hours. Top candidates get a paid trial (10 recorded calls at base rate + show bonuses).





Hiring Process



1. Application (this form)

2. 15-minute interview if your Loom is solid

3. Paid trial (base + bonuses for booked calls) + paid test tasks.

4. Hire decision



No endless interviews. We move fast.



Job Type



Part-time (can become full-time for top performers)



Industry: Marketing / Lead Generation
